title: C# platform developer
description:
**About Us**
 Hacksaw Studios is a Stockholm-based game studio focused on creating innovative, high-quality gaming experiences. With a focus on performance and precision, we bring ideas to life through technical expertise and creative collaboration. We have wide distribution, and our games are played by millions of people every month by players all over the world.
   

  

 We are now looking for a C# platform developer to join our growing Stockholm team. You'll be the person helping build and maintain the high-performance backend systems that power our games and platform infrastructure. You'll be solving real technical challenges that impact thousands of active users, collaborating closely with product teams, and helping ensure our platform is robust and ready for future growth.
   

  

 The Role
   

  

**Your Key Responsibilities Include**
* Design & Implement Features: Build robust, scalable features for our C# / ASP.NET-based platform and supporting systems.
* Optimize Performance: Ensure code and data structures deliver high throughput, low latency, and reliable processing of thousands of transactions per second.
* Testing & Stability: Write and maintain comprehensive unit tests to ensure platform stability.
* Code Collaboration: Participate in code reviews and contribute to clean, maintainable, and well-documented code.
* Technical Documentation: Maintain clear and thorough technical documentation.
* Team Coordination: Work closely with peers and product teams to maintain architectural consistency and meet performance standards.


 What We’re Looking For
   

  

* Strong experience in C# backend development.
* Solid understanding of ASP.NET (experience preferred but can be developed on the job).
* Deep knowledge of SQL, including writing efficient queries and designing high-performance database schemas.
* Practical experience with caching strategies (e.g., Redis).
* Expertise in multithreaded programming and thread-safe design patterns.
* Proficiency in using the C# debugger for troubleshooting complex issues.
* A mindset focused on performance, scalability, and reliability.


**Nice-to-have**
* Experience with automated unit testing frameworks.
* A university degree in Computer Science or related field.
* Interest in gaming, professionally or recreationally.
* Experience with front-end development.
* Experience mentoring or coaching other colleagues.


 What We Offer
   

  

* A modern office in central Stockholm.
* Work with experienced, passionate colleagues in the gaming industry.
* The opportunity to create games with a global audience.
* Real influence, ownership, and personal growth opportunities.
* Wellbeing perks including wellness allowance and regular in-office massage.


 Practical Details
   

  

* This is an on-site position in Stockholm.
* Unfortunately, we cannot assist with relocation or visa sponsorship.


 Ready to Join the Game Makers?
   

  

 Apply now! We review applications on a rolling basis.

title: Software Test Engineer
description:
**About RiskSpectrum** 





 RiskSpectrum develops a suite of desktop and web-based software products used to analyse risk, reliability, and availability in complex systems. Our tools are used in safety-critical industries, including nuclear power, where around 60% of the world’s plants rely on our software.
 





  
 





 Our products are installed in customer-managed environments rather than hosted by us, so testing often involves installations, upgrades, integrations, configurations, and system behaviour across a range of environments.
 





  
 




**The role** 





 This is a hands-on software testing role focused on testing complex software across different environments, configurations, and product integrations. Most testing is manual and exploratory, covering end-to-end workflows, installations, upgrades, regression, and system behaviour across desktop and web-based products.
 





  
 





 The role suits someone who enjoys understanding how software behaves as a whole - across environments, versions, dependencies, and end-to-end system workflows.
 





  
 





 You will be part of a small development team, working closely with developers and product management to help deliver high-quality software in a regulated environment where quality, traceability, and practical thinking matter.
 





  
 





 You will be based in Stockholm, Sweden, working with colleagues in the UK, Germany, and China, with flexible hybrid working and regular in-office collaboration.
 





  
 




**What you’ll do** 





 ·      Work closely with product management and developers to shape and scope new features.
 





 ·      Help refine requirements and design structured, requirement-driven test scenarios.
 





 ·      Execute end-to-end, exploratory, and regression testing across different environments, configurations, and product combinations.
 





 ·      Investigate issues, compare behaviour across versions, and work with developers to resolve them.
 





 ·      Validate installation packages, upgrades, and release integrity.
 





 ·      Help create, maintain, and improve test environments used for development and testing.
 





 ·      Improve how we test, including tooling, automation, and test coverage where it adds value.
 





  
 




**How we work** 





 ·      We are a small, collaborative team working in sprints.
 





 ·      Testers are part of the development process, not a separate phase at the end.
 





 ·      Testing often spans multiple products, including areas where systems and components interact.
 





 ·      Most work is in Windows-based environments, with some Linux.
 





 ·      Some testing involves technically specialised domains, so testers work alongside subject matter experts where needed.
 





  
 




**Tools and technologies** 





 ·      Azure DevOps (Boards, Test Plans, and Pipelines).
 





 ·      VS Code with Gherkin (.feature files).
 





 ·      PowerShell scripting, including Pester for API and regression testing.
 





 ·      C# Playwright testing.
 





 ·      GitHub Copilot and other AI-assisted tools to support test design, coverage, and workflows.
 





 ·      Pipelines, VM images, and environment configurations used to create and maintain test environments.
 





  
 




**What we’re looking for** 





 ·      Commercial experience as a software tester, ideally working with complex scientific, engineering, or other technically complex software.
 





 ·      Experience testing complete workflows, integrated systems, and software behaviour across different environments or configurations.
 





 ·      Confidence in investigating issues, interpreting requirements, and identifying risks, gaps, and edge cases.
 





 ·      Comfortable working in a hands-on, mostly manual testing role focused on installations, upgrades, and system behaviour.
 





 ·      Good communication skills, attention to detail, and a practical, collaborative approach to problem-solving.
 





 ·      Bachelor’s degree in computer science, IT, or a related technical discipline, or equivalent relevant experience.
 





 ·      Currently based in Sweden and with the right to work in Sweden.
 





 ·      Strong written and spoken English.
 










**Nice to have** 




 ·      Experience using Azure DevOps or similar tools for test management and issue tracking.
 



 ·      Familiarity with Gherkin / BDD or structured test design.
 



 ·      Basic scripting experience (e.g. PowerShell or Python).
 



 ·      Exposure to API testing (script-based or tool-based).
 



 ·      Experience using Playwright for end-to-end testing of web apps.

title: Android Software Engineer
description:
**Job Description**

 Are you passionate about creating impactful
 **Mobile experiences on Android** 
 ? We’re looking for a talented and motivated
 **Senior and Mid level Android Software Engineer** 
 to join our team and help shape the future of our mobile products.
   

  





 In this role, you’ll be at the heart of product development, collaborating closely with Product Managers, Engineering Managers, and cross-functional teams. You’ll play a key role in translating business needs into high-quality Android solutions, all while adhering to industry best practices and maintaining clean, scalable code.
   

  





**WHAT YOU’LL DO** 
  

  




* Design, develop, and maintain high-quality Android applications using Kotlin and/or Java.
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define, design, and deliver new features that enhance user experience.
* Write clean, efficient, and testable code following modern Android development best practices.
* Participate in code reviews, architecture discussions, and Agile ceremonies to ensure quality and alignment.
* Debug, troubleshoot, and optimize performance of existing applications to ensure reliability and responsiveness.
* Stay up to date with the latest Android trends, tools, and technologies, and apply them to improve development workflows.
* Ensure applications meet security standards and follow modern compliance practices.
* Contribute to improving internal development processes, tooling, and documentation for better team efficiency.



**WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH** 
  

  





 Our product teams are dedicated to building advanced internal tools that support data-driven decision-making across product development. We focus on analyzing test variations, interpreting performance data, and delivering insights that help product teams optimize feature rollouts. By collaborating closely with cross-functional teams, we ensure that new features are launched efficiently and effectively. Through continuous learning and iteration, we contribute to innovation and excellence in user experience across the organization.
   

  





**WHO YOU ARE**




 We are looking for people with...
   

  




* Professional experience in
 **Android development** 
 . Proficiency in Kotlin (Java is a plus).
* Experience with
 **Jetpack Compose** 
 .
* Solid understanding of Android SDK, Material Design, and Jetpack components.
* Experience working with RESTful APIs, third-party libraries, and JSON.
* Familiarity with Android architectural patterns (e.g., MVVM, Clean Architecture).
* Experience with version control systems, especially Git.
* Knowledge of dependency injection frameworks (e.g., Hilt, Dagger).
* Understanding mobile app performance, memory management, and debugging tools.
* Familiarity with testing frameworks (e.g., JUnit, Espresso).
* Ability to work in an agile development environment.



 Nice to Have
   

  




* Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines for Android.
* Exposure to mobile analytics tools (e.g., Firebase, Mixpanel).
* Published apps in the Google Play Store.



 And people who have:
   

  




* **Problem-Solving Skills** 
 : A logical and creative mindset to tackle challenges and deliver effective solutions, even under pressure.
* **Collaborative Spirit** 
 : The ability to work seamlessly with cross-functional teams including designers, product managers, and backend developers.
* **Interpersonal Communication** 
 : A clear and thoughtful approach to articulating ideas, technical concepts, and feedback, both in writing and verbally.
* **Adaptability** 
 : Comfort with navigating change, learning new tools, and adjusting to evolving project needs.
* **Growth Mindset** 
 : Openness to feedback and a drive to continuously improve and grow professionally.



**WHO WE ARE** 
  

  





 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.
   

  





 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future.
   

  





**WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE** 
  

  





 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe.
   

  




* **Staff discount card** 
 : Usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online.
* **H&M Incentive Program (HIP)** 
 : Included in our HIP. You can read more about our H&M Incentive Program here.
* **Competitive pensions** 
 : Collective Agreement and ITP pensions competitive to the Swedish market.
* **Generous vacation** 
 : 30 days' vacation, health care allowance, and good work-life balance.
* **Additional perks** 
 : Discounts from Benify.
* **Innovative Environment** 
 : Work with cutting-edge technology and innovative solutions.
* **Global Impact** 
 : Be part of a team that influences users worldwide.
* **Professional Growth** 
 : Endless opportunities to learn and develop your skills.
* **Collaborative Culture** 
 : Join a motivated team that values collaboration and excellence.



**JOIN US** 
  

  





 Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.
   

  





 This is a full-time permanent position, starting as soon as possible according to the agreement. The role is a hybrid position, based in our office in Stockholm, Sweden.
   

  





 Apply by sending in your
 **CV in English** 
 as soon as possible. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through the career page.
   

  





 Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here. We are eager to meet you!
   

  





 \*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.

title: iOS Developer
description:
**The Company**
 PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.
   

  

 We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.
   

  

 We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.
   

  

 Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.
   

  

**Job Description Summary:**
 We’re building something new in Stockholm, and we want you to be part of it! We are looking for an iOS Developers to join our Business Experience area, a team dedicated to creating seamless and innovative payment experiences for businesses and their customers. Our solutions serve millions of users globally, and now we’re starting fresh in Stockholm to take things to the next level. This is not just another role – it’s an opportunity to help shape a brand-new team from the ground up. You’ll work in a cross-functional, agile environment, delivering small, high-quality increments that make a real impact. We value collaboration, continuous learning, and supporting each other’s growth in ways that suit your journey. As part of this team, you will: Build modern digital products that improve lives and empower businesses worldwide. Solve interesting and complex problems alongside talented peers. Contribute to a culture of innovation and excellence within PayPal globally. If you’re passionate about creating exceptional user experiences and want to be part of something new and exciting, we’d love to hear from you.
   

  

**Job Description:**
**Essential Responsibilities:**
* Delivers complete solutions spanning all phases of the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) (design, implementation, testing, delivery and operations), based on definitions from more senior roles.
* Advises immediate management on project-level issues
* Guides junior engineers
* Operates with little day-to-day supervision, making technical decisions based on knowledge of internal conventions and industry best practices
* Applies knowledge of technical best practices in making decisions


**Expected Qualifications:**
* 3+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.


**Additional Responsibilities And Preferred Qualifications**
* Good experience in iOS development, with good knowledge of Swift.
* Familiarity with iOS SDK, SwiftUI and various Apple's frameworks and understand how to leverage them effectively to build robust iOS applications.
* Understanding of common architectural patterns such as MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel).
* Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and automation tools like Jenkins or Fastlane to streamline the build, test, and deployment process.
* Commitment to writing clean, maintainable code with a focus on code quality and experience with unit testing, integration testing, and UI testing.
* Keen interest in keeping up to date with the latest technological development.
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.


 PayPal does not charge candidates any fees for courses, applications, resume reviews, interviews, background checks, or onboarding. Any such request is a red flag and likely part of a scam. To learn more about how to identify and avoid recruitment fraud please visit https://careers.pypl.com/contact-us.
   

  

 For the majority of employees, PayPal's balanced hybrid work model offers 3 days in the office for effective in-person collaboration and 2 days at your choice of either the PayPal office or your home workspace, ensuring that you equally have the benefits and conveniences of both locations.
   

  

**Our Benefits:**
 At PayPal, we’re committed to building an equitable and inclusive global economy. And we can’t do this without our most important asset-you. That’s why we offer benefits to help you thrive in every stage of life. We champion your financial, physical, and mental health by offering valuable benefits and resources to help you care for the whole you.
   

  

 We have great benefits including a flexible work environment, employee shares options, health and life insurance and more. To learn more about our benefits please visit https://www.paypalbenefits.com
   

  

**Who We Are:**
 To learn more about our culture and community visit https://about.pypl.com/who-we-are/default.aspx
   

  

**Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion**
 PayPal provides equal employment opportunity (EEO) to all persons regardless of age, color, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, race, religion, creed, gender, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, genetic information, marital status, status with regard to public assistance, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, PayPal will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you are unable to submit an application because of incompatible assistive technology or a disability, please contact us at paypalglobaltalentacquisition@paypal.com.
   

  

**Belonging at PayPal:**
 Our employees are central to advancing our mission, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can do their best work with a sense of purpose and belonging. Belonging at PayPal means creating a workplace with a sense of acceptance and security where all employees feel included and valued. We are proud to have a diverse workforce reflective of the merchants, consumers, and communities that we serve, and we continue to take tangible actions to cultivate inclusivity and belonging at PayPal.
   

  

 Any general requests for consideration of your skills, please Join our Talent Community.
   

  

 We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates. Please don’t hesitate to apply.
   

  

 REQ ID R0131984

title: Software Engineer - Billing Platform
description:
**Introducing evroc**
 At evroc, we are building a secure, sovereign, and sustainable hyperscale cloud to reimagine the digital future of Europe. By joining our company, you have an exciting opportunity to contribute to the development of next-generation cloud services. We are seeking intellectually curious and highly motivated team members who are ready to embrace the thrilling challenge of building the first European owned hyperscale cloud.
   

  

**Location:** 
 Stockholm
   

  

**Job type:** 
 Permanent position
   

  

**Join the Pioneers**
 We’re looking for a Software Engineer to build the core of evroc’s Billing Platform, a system that processes millions of events and powers usage-based pricing, invoicing, and revenue flows across our cloud.
   

  

 This is a hybrid role across backend engineering and data engineering. You will own the full lifecycle of billing data, from ingestion and processing to storage and reconciliation, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and scalability across both real-time and batch systems.
   

  

 You’ll work on the systems that ensure that every usage event is captured, transformed, and reflected correctly in billing, at scale, and with zero tolerance for errors.
   

  

**What You’ll Tackle**
* Design and build end-to-end billing data pipelines, from event ingestion to analytical storage.
* Develop backend services and APIs handling validation, processing, and billing logic in distributed systems.
* Implement event-driven architectures with strong guarantees around idempotency and financial consistency.
* Design data models and storage strategies that support both high-throughput ingestion and efficient querying.
* Build data processing jobs that enrich usage data with pricing, entitlements, and cost calculations.
* Implement reconciliation and validation mechanisms to ensure data accuracy across systems.
* Integrate billing systems with external financial platforms such as payment providers and accounting systems.
* Build observability into pipelines and services, ensuring visibility into data freshness, throughput, and correctness.


**Skills We Value**
* 5+ years of experience in backend engineering and/or data engineering within complex systems.
* Strong experience with Golang and building distributed, event-driven systems.
* Experience working with message buses such as Kafka, Pub/Sub or similar.
* Hands-on experience with data processing frameworks and building ETL/ELT pipelines.
* Strong database knowledge across both relational and NoSQL systems, and understanding of consistency trade-offs.
* Experience designing data models and handling schema evolution in large-scale systems.
* Familiarity with Kubernetes and cloud-native infrastructure.
* Understanding of financial systems, billing, or accounting principles is a strong plus.


**Why Join evroc?**
 We offer a competitive salary and an equity package to attract the best. Whether it’s the cosmopolitan allure of London, the vibrant Stockholm, or the Mediterranean charm of Sophia Antipolis, you’re poised for an inspiring work environment and a captivating local culture!
   

  

 At evroc, diversity is our strength. We champion an inclusive environment where every background - ethnicity, age, gender identity, beliefs, and culture - is celebrated.
   

  

 Applicants must possess a valid work permit.

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
We are looking for a native Swedish speaking Platform Engineer to join a Stockholm based consulting company.
 



 The role requires up to 3 days per week onsite.
 



  





 In this role, you will work hands-on with teams to design, build, and improve cloud-native platforms and workflows. Your responsibilities may include:
 


* Designing, building, and operating Kubernetes-based platforms
* Developing and optimising CI/CD pipelines for faster, safer deployments
* Implementing Infrastructure as Code to enable scalable and repeatable environments
* Enhancing developer workflows and enabling self-service capabilities
* Establishing monitoring and observability solutions for improved system insight
* Advising on cloud architecture, best practices, and engineering standards



 You’ll typically work within cross-functional, product-driven teams building modern, distributed systems in the cloud.
 



  





**Technology Stack** 




 You’ll frequently work with a range of modern tools and platforms, including:
 


* Kubernetes
* Terraform
* Cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
* Docker
* CI/CD tools such as GitHub Actions or GitLab CI
* Monitoring tools like Prometheus and Grafana



 Experience with GitOps tools such as ArgoCD or Flux is a plus.
 



  





**What We’re Looking For** 




 We’re looking for someone who combines strong technical expertise with a passion for improving how teams build and deliver software. Ideally, you have:
 


* Several years of experience in cloud infrastructure or DevOps roles
* Strong hands-on experience with Kubernetes and container platforms
* Proven experience with Infrastructure as Code (e.g. Terraform)
* Experience designing or maintaining CI/CD pipelines
* Solid understanding of Linux systems and networking fundamentals



 Bonus points if you have experience in platform engineering, developer experience, or working with large-scale distributed systems.

Our capabilities span integrated operations, transformation, and business AI — enabling new ways of working, new productivity paradigms, and new roads to value. title: Backend Game Developer
description:
**iGaming:**
 If it’s iGaming, we know a thing or two. At Light & Wonder, It’s all about the games, and our digital team leads the global iGaming market, providing jaw-dropping content and innovative software that powers the world's most successful online gaming providers.
   

  

**Position Summary**
**Backend Game Developer – Shape the Future of iGaming**
 Light & Wonder | Full-Time
   

  

 Are you a seasoned C++ developer with a passion for building high-performance, scalable systems? Do you want your code to power real-money gaming experiences played by millions across the globe? We're looking for a Backend Game Developer to join our team and help us push the boundaries of what's possible in the international online eGaming market.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
 You'll be a cornerstone of our backend development team — not just writing code, but shaping how we build. From game logic and APIs to BackOffice administration tools, your fingerprints will be on the systems that keep our platform running smoothly and competitively.
   

  

* Design and develop robust backend game logic, APIs, and configuration systems
* Drive architectural decisions and tech stack choices that keep us ahead of the curve
* Ensure cross-platform compatibility across all devices, channels, and markets
* Proactively manage and reduce technical debt to keep our Game Server stable and performant
* Leverage AI tools — including large language models like Claude by Anthropic — to accelerate development, improve code quality, and explore innovative game features
* Maintain clear, thorough documentation as a standard — not an afterthought


**Qualifications**
**What We're Looking For**
* Several years of backend development experience or equivalent, with strong C++ expertise
* Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science or equivalent
* Solid grasp of HTTP, XML, JSON, and web-based API design
* Comfortable working in Microsoft Windows environments; Visual Studio experience is a plus
* Experience with cloud platforms such as Azure or AWS; .NET Core familiarity is a bonus
* Curiosity about and hands-on experience with AI-assisted development workflows — using tools like Claude, GitHub Copilot, or similar to write better code faster
* Strong communicator in English — clear, direct, and collaborative
* Structured and methodical, with a natural instinct for ownership and leadership


 At Light & Wonder, you won't just maintain a system — you'll help evolve it. You'll have real influence over the technology decisions that define our platform, work alongside talented engineers in a fast-moving industry, and be at the forefront of integrating AI into game development workflows. The iGaming space moves fast, and so do we.
   

  

**Additional Information**
**Why would you enjoy working with us at Light & Wonder?**
* Competitive benefits, an open and supportive environment as well as a modern and exciting workplace
* The opportunity to interact with global teams on a regular basis
* Tangible and genuine development - at Light & Wonder, you can take your career where you want it to go!


**And if that’s not enough** 
 ; you will get to enjoy a
 **stunning work location** 
 and
 **flexible working practices** 
 whilst we provide you with the
 **guidance** 
 and
 **development skills** 
 you need to progress quickly and
 **enhance your career.**

title: Business Developer
description:
**Location:** 

 Stockholm (Hybrid) or Remote
 




**Start Date:** 

 April–May
 




**Seniority Level:** 

 Mid to Senior
 




**Contract Type:** 

 Freelance Contract
 




**Work Hours:** 

 Must overlap at least 6 hours between 13:00–21:00 CET, including mandatory availability from 18:00–20:00 CET.
 




  




**About the Role** 

  






 We are looking for a proactive and relationship-driven Business Developer to help expand Envar Studio’s client base and build strong, lasting partnerships within the gaming industry.
 





 This role is focused on long-term growth. We are looking for someone who is not only excited to drive new business opportunities, but also interested in growing with the studio over time and becoming a stable, trusted part of Envar’s journey.
 





 You will take ownership of identifying opportunities, opening doors, developing tailored pitches, and representing Envar externally across the industry. You will work closely with leadership and production teams to ensure a smooth transition from sales to delivery, while helping create a strong and professional client experience throughout the full partnership lifecycle.
 





 A big part of the role also happens outside meeting rooms, from conferences and networking events to the more informal side of the industry, like social gatherings and after parties, where some of the best connections tend to happen.
 





 This role is a strong fit for someone who understands the game development landscape, brings a solid industry network or the ability to build one quickly, and can balance relationship-building with structure, follow-through, and high-quality execution.
 




  




**Responsibilities** 

  




* Identify, approach, and develop new client opportunities through a range of outbound business development channels
* Conduct market research and competitor analysis to inform outreach strategy and prepare tailored proposals
* Build, maintain, and grow strong relationships with prospective and existing clients by understanding their needs, goals, and challenges
* Represent Envar at industry events, studio visits, business meetings, and conferences both virtually and in person, as well as the more informal side of the industry, including networking mixers and after parties where relationships often continue to grow
* Prepare project estimates, sales materials, and pitches aligned with Envar’s service offering and cost structure
* Maintain a clean, accurate, and up-to-date sales pipeline within the CRM system
* Work closely with production teams to ensure timely, efficient, and well-documented handovers
* Adapt to established workflows while maintaining clear communication across all relevant internal and external stakeholders
* Act as a consistent and professional representative of Envar’s brand, values, and quality standards in all client-facing interactions



  




**Qualifications** 

  




* Minimum 2 years of experience working in a Business Developer role within the video game industry
* Good understanding of the art production pipeline for video games
* Strong communication and relationship-building skills, with the ability to professionally represent Envar’s brand and values
* Strong research, planning, and organizational skills, with the ability to work independently and maintain a high standard of execution
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
* An existing network in the games industry, or clear ability to build and develop one effectively
* Availability and readiness to travel internationally for industry events and meetings when needed, sometimes on short notice
* Willingness to occasionally work outside standard hours and balance this with flex time
* Interest in a long-term collaboration, with the intention to commit and grow alongside the studio



  




**Bonus Points** 

  




* Experience within the
 
**AA and/or AAA** 

 games market
* Previous experience attending, networking at, or representing a studio at game industry events
* Professional working proficiency in languages other than English
* Openness to the possibility of relocation in the future, depending on role and business needs
* A genuine passion for games and the game development industry



  




**Why Join Envar?** 

  






 At Envar, you will have real ownership, trust, and the chance to play a meaningful role in how the studio grows. You will work closely with talented teams, represent the studio across the global games industry, and help build long-term partnerships that lead to exciting creative work.
 





 We are looking for someone who wants to make a real impact and stay for the long run, someone who values autonomy, responsibility, and being part of building something lasting.

title: Embedded Developer Automotive
description:
**Want to work with automotive development – but not expected to know everything?**
 At Knowit Connectivity, we’re growing within Automotive and are looking for more engineers to join us. What we’ve learned is that great people don’t always fit into one single box – so instead of looking for one “perfect” profile, we’re interested in people with different kinds of experience.
   

  

 Maybe you enjoy working close to the hardware with C/C++ in embedded systems.
   

  

 Maybe you prefer model-based development in Simulink.
   

  

 Or maybe testing, verification, or system-level work is where you feel at home.
   

  

 We’d like to hear from you regardless.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a consultant with us, you’ll be part of a team where collaboration and knowledge sharing are important. Our projects vary, and so does the way we work – sometimes onsite with the customer, sometimes in-house, sometimes remote.
   

  

 We’re mainly working with customers in the Stockholm region within Automotive.
   

  

 We put a strong focus on competence development – not just through courses, but by building knowledge together. We want you to grow in your role and stay relevant over time.
   

  

 Right now, we’re putting extra focus on areas like AI and how it can be applied within automotive and embedded systems. This isn’t just something we talk about – it’s something we actively explore in our projects and internally, and you’ll have the opportunity to be part of that journey.
   

  

**We think you might have experience in things like:**
* C/C++ or model-based development (Simulink)
* Embedded systems and/or Linux
* Testing (unit, integration, system) or verification
* Automotive standards like ISO26262 or A-SPICE


 It’s great if you also know things like CAN, Autosar, Python or real-time systems – but it’s not a must.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 You probably enjoy working together with others, take responsibility for your work, and are curious about learning new things.
   

  

 You have an interest in how technology is evolving and are open to exploring new areas,
   

  

 We also think you like sharing knowledge with others and contributing to a team where people learn from each other. We don’t expect you to know everything – but we do expect you to want to grow.
   

  

**About Us**
 Knowit offers an exciting workplace that provides great opportunities for our employees to develop, both professionally and personally. With supportive, versatile, and creative colleagues, we at Knowit Connectivity Stockholm offer an exciting and inclusive culture where we strive to ensure a healthy balance between private life and professional life. In addition to complementing each other professionally, we also make sure we have fun together, both inside and outside of office hours, with a variety of events such as workshops, informative seminars and casual after-works.
   

  

 All our leaders have a great focus on our employees and through a close dialogue with you, we promise to do our utmost to make the next project the best of your life so far. We are a company that values a culture of closeness and community. We listen when you have suggestions for improvements or new ideas for events and will always encourage you to try new solutions. Knowit is a fast-paced, fun, and inspiring workplace with a great team spirit. With us, you work to make a difference and to develop both Knowit’ s business and our customers.

title: Dynamics 365 CE Developer
description:
**Dynamics 365 CE Developer, Major European Omnichannel Retail Programme (Remote)** 




  





 A leading European omnichannel retailer is delivering one of the largest and most complex Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement programmes in the region. As part of this long‑term digitalisation initiative, the organisation is seeking a Dynamics 365 CE Technical Owner to take full technical responsibility for a highly sophisticated and business‑critical CE environment.
 



  





**About the Role** 




  





 This position provides complete technical ownership of an extensive D365 CE landscape operating at significant scale. The Technical Owner will act as the organisation’s primary authority on D365 CE architecture, integrations and platform evolution, supported by a strong and established internal Dynamics team.
 



  





 The role is offered on a
 **fully remote basis - but candidates have to be based in Sweden** 
 , with the additional flexibility to work from the company’s modern office whenever desired.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




  




* Serve as the technical authority for the global D365 CE environment, ensuring stability, performance, and long‑term scalability.
* Lead technical decision‑making, platform design, and continuous improvement of the CE ecosystem.
* Oversee integrations, solution architecture, environments, and the technical lifecycle of key components.
* Work closely with functional specialists, developers, and business stakeholders to translate technology direction into actionable delivery.
* Collaborate with multiple well‑known ISVs aligned to the retail industry, owning technical governance and solution alignment.
* Maintain oversight of release management, technical refinement, and system optimisation.



  





**What This Opportunity Offers** 




  




* Full technical ownership of one of Europe’s most advanced D365 CE implementations.
* A central role in a major omnichannel retail digitalisation programme with significant scale and complexity.
* Variety and depth through collaboration with several industry‑leading ISV partners.
* A supportive, knowledgeable, and well‑established Dynamics team surrounding the role.
* Fully remote work in Sweden with the option to use the office at any time.
* A competitive employment package including collective agreement benefits and a home office allowance.



  





**Candidate Profile** 




  





 The ideal candidate brings deep technical expertise in Dynamics 365 CE, strong architectural capability, and experience guiding complex enterprise‑level environments. They should be comfortable acting as the go‑to authority, making sound technical decisions, and influencing the long‑term digital direction of a large, technology‑driven organisation.
 



  





 Interested? Apply now or contact Eddie on e.leith@tenthrevolution.com

title: Data & AI Business Developer
description:
We are expanding our Data & AI Business Development team. We’ve already seen the massive impact data & AI can have on our business and now, we’re looking for a hands-on Business Developer to join our team and help us accelerate that journey.
   

  

 We are looking for a person who is motivated by tangible business outcomes and enjoys working closely with people across the business to turn ideas into measurable business value.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Create the "Why" behind our initiatives. You’ll use data and SQL to validate ideas and ensure we are prioritizing the projects with the highest potential impact.
* Drive the end-to-end journey of a data & AI initiative, from early discovery and feasibility assessment to final rollout, impact evaluation and continuous improvement.
* Work closely with people from different teams across Lendo Group to find where data & AI can have significant business impact.
* Act as the vital link & translator between our Data Science team, Product & Tech teams and our Business units, ensuring alignment and efficient execution.
* Contribute with your insights from the field to help shape the long-term strategy for the Group.

title: Engineering Manager
description:
**WHO WE ARE** 





 At Trustly, we're building a smarter, faster, and more secure financial future by revolutionizing the world of payments. As a global leader in Open Banking Payments, we are establishing Pay by Bank as the new standard at checkout, providing unparalleled freedom, speed, and ease to millions of consumers and merchants worldwide.
 




  






 Our Ambition: To build the world’s most disruptive payment network and redefine what the payment experience should feel like.
 




  






 Trustly is a global team of innovators, collaborators, and doers.  If you are driven by a strong sense of purpose and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial, and high-growth environment, join us and be part of a team that’s transforming the way the world pays.
 




  





**About the Product & Tech Organisation** 



  


  
 


  



 Our Product & Technology organisation drives the development of Trustly’s products. The cross functional teams collectively work with innovative ideas, development of products and platform and set the vision and strategy for the problem spaces they own. The Product & Tech organisation consists of a bit more than 200 people spread across Stockholm, Lisbon, Örebro, Paris, London and Izmir. 
 



  


  
 


  



 All teams consist of a product manager, an engineering manager, a lead developer and engineers. Some teams also have designers and analytics competence in the team. They all work closely together to enable new products and features within existing products within Europe and to make sure the payment platform runs without interruptions and with high performance.
 



  


  
 


  



 We are now looking to strengthen our engineering capacity within Product & Technology with one more senior position. We operate in an agile culture combined with DevOps, allowing our teams to take on full responsibility from code to on-call.
 



  


  
 


  


**About the Role** 



  


  
 


  



 As an
 
**Engineering Manager** 

 at Trustly, you will play a crucial role in leading and supporting an agile team of talented engineers. Working closely with product managers, lead developers, and other stakeholders, you’ll ensure delivery of high-quality, scalable, and reliable systems while fostering a positive and empowering team culture. This is an excellent opportunity to bridge technical excellence and people management, delivering real impact in the payments industry.
 





  




**What you'll do** 
  
* **Team Leadership** 
 : Lead, coach, and support a team of engineers, enabling them to perform at their best while fostering a culture of collaboration, ownership, and continuous learning
  
* **Delivery Excellence** 
 : Drive successful delivery of the team’s commitments, ensuring alignment with domain objectives and product goals
  
* **Technical Collaboration** 
 : Work hands-on with lead developers and engineers to design, implement, and maintain scalable systems and solutions aligned with Trustly’s technical standards
  
* **Product Partnership** 
 : Work closely with the product manager to define and prioritise the team’s roadmap, balancing short-term goals with long-term vision
  
* **Operational Ownership** 
 : Ensure your team’s systems operate reliably in production, with high performance, availability, and maintainability
  
* **Talent Development** 
 : Recruit, onboard, and mentor engineers, creating an environment where they can grow and thrive
  
* **Process Improvement** 
 : Advocate for and implement best practices in agile methodologies, DevOps, and engineering processes to continuously improve team effectiveness
  

  



  




**Who you are** 
  
* Proven experience managing engineering teams in agile environments, with a strong focus on team empowerment and delivery
  
* Hands-on engineering background with expertise in Java and experience in building scalable, robust systems
  
* Familiarity with modern tech stacks including Kubernetes, Docker, Kafka, and Spring Boot
  
* Experience working with cloud solutions (preferably AWS), monitoring, and analytics
  
* Knowledge of service-based architecture and microservices
  
* Strong ability to balance technical decisions with business needs, ensuring delivery without sacrificing scalability, performance, or reliability
  
* Committed to fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, and excellence
  
* Passion for DevOps practices and ownership, with a track record of driving improvements in automation and monitoring
  
* Exceptional communication, problem-solving, and leadership skills
  
* Fluent in English
  

  



  




**At Trustly, you’ll have the opportunity to:** 
  
* Shape the future of payments as part of a global leader in the industry
  
* Work with cutting-edge technology and highly skilled colleagues in an agile and collaborative environment
  
* Take on a role with real impact, where your contributions directly drive the success of your team and Trustly’s mission
  
* Enjoy a supportive and dynamic culture that encourages growth, innovation, and ownership
  

  






  




  


**Our Fantastic Benefits**

 (varies by location)
 




 🌴
 **20 to 30 days of holiday** 
 to support a healthy work-life balance
 



 🥳
 **Monthly team outing allowance** 
 to enjoy social events with your colleagues
 



 👶
 **Parental leave top-up** 
 additional support for new parents
 



 🥐
 **Daily breakfast and on-site perks** 
 to make your workday smoother
 



 💚
 **Well being support** 
 our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best
 



**PLUS** 
 additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!
 



  





**Shape Your Role and Make an Impact**




 Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.
 



 If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you!
 **Apply now and submit your CV in English.** 



  


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Senior Battery researcher and business developer
description:
*Are you passionate about electrochemistry, especially lithium-ion batteries, sodium ion batteries and energy storage devices? Do you also want to be part of RISE's journey as an innovation partner in the field of batteries? Do you have prior experience from working in or closely with battery manufacturing industry. If so, you might be the one we are looking for!*
**About Us**
 RISE is an important player in energy storage technology. With a relentless focus on innovation, RISE supports numerous industries in accelerating their innovations toward an electrified future. At RISE, we work in a dynamic environment where problem-solving is an important component of all work. With R&D facilities in Stockholm, RISE is the perfect platform to have an impact and support industry by designing the next-generation battery products.
   

  

**About The Role**
 To grow our business, we are now looking for a battery R&D senior researcher/Bussiness developer to the unit Advanced materials and binders. In this role, you will have opportunities to accelerate and develop over time. The role largely includes research and participation in customer projects and research projects, as well as writing proposals and quotes to research funders and industrial partners. You will also play a large role in the business development of the battery area, and you will have close contact with customers and research partners to identify new projects within our focus areas. You will need to be flexible in this role as you will work in several projects simultaneously.
   

  

 This is a permanent position, located in Stockholm.
   

  

**What will you be doing?**
 Cycle aging and diagnostics. Preparation and assembly of lithium-ion batteries from slurry mixing to spreading and cell assembly. Perform/lead in-house machinery tuning, maintenance and function of the preparation and assembly systems. Take part/lead in scale-up process development, including data extraction and analysis according to the team’s needs. Lead/Support and participate in method development and validation for postmortem analysis. From time to time, traveling to company manufacturing sites (locally and overseas) according to the company needs and tasks in accordance with the role. Create and maintain relationship with companies. Lead large European projects with 10-20 stakeholders. Generate offers, write proposals and quotes and perform sales activities. Network and lead workshops. Be partly involved in the market organization with focus on business development for the battery area.
   

  

**Who are you?**
 To succeed in this role you need to enjoy working in a structured way, often in several different projects at the same time, and in close collaboration with customers and research partners. You are a team-player approach with a business minded approach and are able to understand the customer's perspective.
   

  

 You also need to have the following requirements:
   

  

* PhD in Engineering (e.g., Process Engineering, Battery Materials Engineering – cathode/anode active materials, Battery Specialist, Electrochemistry) with a minimum of 3 years of industrial experience in or closely related to the battery industry (More years in the battery industry are beneficial),


 or MSc in a relevant field with at least 10 years of industrial experience in the battery industry
   

  

* Alternatively, an academic background equivalent to Professor, Associate/Assistant Professor, or Docent with an interest in moving into applied battery science
* Hands-on experience from battery laboratory, pilot-scale, or production environments
* Solid understanding of battery materials and cell design to meet customer and application requirements
* Strong documentation practices and structured reporting skills
* Established network within the battery industry
* Experience in developing business proposals and offers for innovative bilateral or collaborative projects
* Good understanding of the needs of the battery industry and society from a battery perspective
* Experience with cell teardown and glovebox work is considered an advantage
* Knowledge of national and European battery regulations and directives, such as the Battery Passport and the European Critical Raw Materials Act
* Fluent in spoken and written English


**Are we right for each other?**
 At RISE, we like different things, and we are convinced that diversity contributes to an innovative environment where we together challenge boundaries and develop new knowledge and skills for the future. With us, passionate problem solvers meet to solve some of the world's most important, and perhaps funniest, problems. We can't promise you an easy job, but what we can promise you is a bunch of dedicated colleagues and some really exciting societal challenges to address and develop opportunities both professionally and personally. With us, you will have the opportunity to make a real difference. Welcome to the entire Swedish research institute and innovation partners!
   

  

**Welcome with your application!**
 If this sounds interesting and you want to know more, you are welcome contact recruiting manager Juhanes Aydin, Head of Unit Binder and Additive, phone 010-516 6387. You are welcome to submit your application before 12th of April 2026. Selection and interviews may take place on an ongoing basis during the application period.
   

  

 Our union representatives are Ingemar Petermann, SACO, +46 10 228 41 22 and Linda Ikatti, Unionen, +46 10 516 51 61. Calls from external recruitment companies and sellers were kindly excluded.

title: Account Manager
description:
We're on the lookout for a high-performing and humble Account Manager with a strong tech background and interest to play a pivotal role in our rapid expansion within the Global Enterprise team. As one of Europe's fastest-growing companies in the industry, expanding remains a key strategic goal.
 



  





 As part of the Account Management team, you’ll be responsible for growing our business with existing clients as well as initiating long-term collaborations between clients and developers.
 



  





**What your week will look like:** 




  




* Lead client meetings to gain an in-depth understanding of their business and development needs, effectively pitch Proxify, and secure the next steps to matching them with their next developer.
* Drive deals through the sales funnel once qualified - ensuring speed and quality.
* Work closely with the Client Engineering team to carefully match the opportunity with the right developer, ensuring their skills and experience are the right fit for client requirements.
* Close deals, onboard clients and nurture strong client relationships.
* Continuously contribute to improving our ways of working and increase efficiency and success as we grow and evolve.



  





**Why you’ll love this opportunity at Proxify:** 




  





 🌐 Solve most tech teams’ biggest challenge: bridging their competence gap.
 



 ❤️ Join a team of tech lovers where support and humility are our guiding principles.
 



 🚀 Great opportunities for professional development and personal growth.
 



 ✈ Work in an international environment (our team spanning 39 countries!).
 



  





**To be successful in this role, we believe that you:** 




  




* Understand how to help a client and guiding them through the customer journey
* Are not just a talker; you make things happen and pay keen attention to details!
* You are ambitious, willing to work hard and walk the extra mile.
* Have hands-on experience with account manager within technology, developer, or a similar role, ideally within a tech team
* Have an intrinsic sense of urgency, responsibility and ownership.
* Possess a curiosity about technological innovations and trends, continually seeking to understand how they can be leveraged to solve real-world problems and improve efficiency.



  





**Why it may not be your match:** 




  





 ❌ You like everyday to look the same (clear routines and process), this might not be for you because we build as we go and keep agile.
 



 ❌ You don’t have any technical experience and not a curious personality.
 



 ❌ You are passively waiting for tasks and work to be handed to you.
 



  





 Do you see yourself thriving in this role and making a huge impact?
 



 If so, we can't wait to meet you!
 



  





*Kindly submit your application and CV in English, as this is the standardised language we use at Proxify.*

title: Technical Writer
description:
***Do you want to be part of creating fantastic, unique and entertaining iGaming experiences in a competitive global market?***




***We are looking for a structured and detail-driven Technical Writer with a passion for clarity and precision to join our iGaming studio in Stockholm. ELK Studios is a leading game developer in the iGaming industry, known for our innovative, visually stunning, and critically acclaimed game titles and IPs. We push the boundaries of gameplay and visual fidelity, setting new standards in the iGaming world.***




  





**ABOUT THE ROLE** 









 As a Technical Writer, you will ensure that our games are clearly communicated to players and fully compliant across regulated markets worldwide. You will take on the responsibility for game rules, documentation standards, and supporting our production flow to ensure accuracy in every detail.
 



  





**A typical work week as a Technical Writer consists of:** 




  




* Writing and maintaining game rules for our slot games while ensuring clarity and regulatory compliance. You will also define and uphold high writing standards and tone across all game-related content.
* Update and adapt existing games to meet requirements for new and regulated markets, which includes certifications and necessary rule adjustments.
* Collaborate across disciplines such as Product Owners, Developers, QA, and Compliance teams to ensure all documentation is accurate and aligned with the game’s functionality.
* Support our Producers by helping manage documentation, requirements, and communication flows throughout the development process.



  





**WHO YOU ARE** 




  





 We believe you are a person who thrives on structure and has an exceptional eye for detail. You are passionate about clear communication and take pride in ensuring that complex logic is translated into easy-to-understand rules. You excel at maintaining consistency and adapting your writing style to meet both creative and regulatory needs.
 



 To thrive with us, you need to be a team player with a good ability to work together with different disciplines. You are self-driven and responsible, with a strong sense of ownership over your work. It is important that you are organized and solution-oriented, with the ability to tackle challenges and deliver on deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
 



  





**QUALIFICATIONS** 




  




* Proven experience as a Technical Writer, ideally within iGaming, casino, or other regulated industries.
* Excellent English skills (written and oral) with a strong focus on clarity and precision is essential. Proficiency in Swedish is a plus but not required.
* Deep understanding of structured documentation and content standards.
* A high attention to detail, especially regarding rule logic and consistency across different platforms and markets.
* Ability to work cross-functionally in fast-paced production environments



  





**NICE-TO-HAVES** 




  




* Experience writing slot game rules or paytable descriptions
* Knowledge about regulated markets such as Malta, UK, Sweden
* Understanding of compliance requirements and certification process
* Experience of working within agile team



  





**WE OFFER** 




  





 We offer an exciting opportunity to work on innovative gaming projects. With our short project cycles, you will have the chance to contribute to many different titles and play a vital part in creating immersive iGaming experiences. Join us in a vibrant and innovative environment where your expertise in documentation and communication will truly make a difference.
 



 We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
 



 We are a creative and collaborative company and we work together on-site at our great office in the heart of the city, just two minutes from T-Centralen, Stockholm. Here, we are right in the middle of the action with Stockholm’s best commute and the pulse of the city right outside the door.
 



 We will fill the position as soon as we have found the right candidate, so do not hesitate to submit your application as soon as possible.
 



  





**Please note that ELK Studios does not offer visa sponsorship or relocation assistance!** 




**This role is fully on-site.**

title: Global API Product Manager
description:
Are you ready to shape the future of digital insurance?
   

  

 As a Global API Product Manager at If, you’ll be at the heart of our digital transformation, driving and enabling new business opportunities and great customer experiences through APIs, IAM and AI.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As our new Global API Product Manager, you will be the main business responsible for processes and technologies for API Products across our business areas and countries.
   

  

 You get to collaborate with professionals in business and IT organizations to ensure that the business gets the API Products and related services needed. Providing guidance on best practices to internal and external teams, that are to expose or consume APIs, will be part of your daily work.
   

  

 Your role is central to cross‑functional initiatives, delivering new API Products and related capabilities, and promoting API‑first thinking across the organization. You’ll also be seen as a forerunner in understanding AI Agents, and how APIs/MCPs, IAM, data and tooling shape the way AI solutions are built and scaled.
   

  

**About The Team**
 You’ll join Digital Sales and Customer Experience, within Business Area Private, focusing on digital enablers for transformation. Our team lives by If’s core values - collaborating across business and IT to deliver the API Products our business needs. We believe in creativity, courage, and smart investments to meet future customer demands.
   

  

**Who Are You?**
 You are an experienced product manager at heart, that can apply product management principles and thinking to products, processes or new technologies. You are a natural leader that thrives in the intersection between business and IT, building trust across stakeholder groups. You are curious and not afraid to roll up your sleeves to drive change in a structured and result-oriented manner.
   

  

**As our new Global API Product Manager, you will be responsible for:**
* Strategy & Product Leadership
+ Own the business strategy for API Products in If and align it with other relevant strategies. Collaborate with representatives in Private, Commercial and Industrial and IT to align with their strategies
+ Manage the API product portfolio in collaboration with API Product Managers
+ Prepare for and shape If’s position on Open Insurance and upcoming regulations
+ Prepare for AI agents and participate in related strategies and product development.

* Governance, Processes & Compliance
+ Own processes, tools, and governance mechanisms for developing and managing API products
+ Ensure alignment across API, IAM, AI, security, legal, and regulatory compliance

* Platform, Developer Experience & AI Enablement
+ Own the developer portal experience, including APIs, MCPs, and AI agent interfaces and related flows
+ Define end‑to‑end patterns for how APIs, MCPs, and AI agents work together
+ Act as business owner for developer enablement, including standards for API and AI agent consumption

* Enablement & Adoption
+ Guide and support teams producing or consuming APIs across the organization
+ Drive organizational learning and adoption through training and communication


**You Also Have:**
* Experience with formal or informal leadership in an agile, user-centric and iterative environment; servant leadership and the ability to empower teams and scale ways of working
* Solid experience in Product Management, in the nexus between business and IT
* Knowledge of API Products, API Architecture and API Security
* Understanding of AI applications, AI agents and their integration with APIs
* Experience working in complex organizational and technical setups
* Ability to easily build relations and trust in stakeholder groups across business and technical functions
* Strong drive and a structured and result-oriented way of working
* Excellent communication skills and ability to drive change
* Strong drive and a structured, result-oriented approach
* Fluent in English (written and oral). Nordic language skills are a plus
* Solid educational background, in either technology or business, higher education degree


**Additional Facts And The Recruitment Process**
**Application deadline:** 
 Last day to apply is 15.04.2026. Screening and interviewing will start immediately.
   

**To apply for the position:** 
 Please attach your CV and answer the questions in the recruitment system. No cover letter needed.
   

**Work location** 
 : Oslo, Stockholm, Gothenburg, Espoo, Turku, or Riga.
   

**Travelling** 
 : Required in the Nordic & Baltic countries, around 6 times a year.
   

**Start** 
 : Upon agreement.
   

  

 For more information, please contact Elisabeth Falck, Head of Digital Business Enablers, on mobile +47 915 36 363.
   

  

*Background checks will be done in accordance with the law in the country of employment.*
 We look forward to receiving your application!

title: API Product Manager
description:
Are you ready to shape the future of digital insurance?
   

  

 As an API Product Manager at If, you’ll be at the heart of our digital transformation, enabling new business opportunities and great customer experiences through APIs, IAM and AI.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are looking for an API Product Manager to join our team. In this role you will impact the governance and processes around the development and lifecycle management of API Products across our business areas and countries. Your work ensures our APIs are secure, scalable, and aligned with evolving business needs, including AI integration and compliance with IAM and security standards.
   

  

 You’ll collaborate closely with developers, architects, legal, and business stakeholders to enable teams to deliver robust and compliant APIs. You’ll guide teams in producing and consuming APIs, lead risk assessments for external partners, and create engaging content for our Developer Portal.
   

  

 Your role is central to cross‑functional initiatives, delivering new API Products and capabilities, and promoting API‑first thinking across the organization. You’ll be seen as a forerunner in understanding AI Agents, agentic processes and how APIs, data, and access controls shape the way AI solutions are built and scaled.
   

  

 The role will initially cover for a colleague going on maternity leave and is expected to evolve over time, with responsibilities gradually shifting towards product management for AI-driven use cases in the API and IAM area, including the data and tools accessed by AI agents.
   

  

**About The Team**
 You’ll join Digital Sales and Customer Experience, within Business Area Private, focusing on digital enablers for transformation. Our team lives by If’s core values - collaborating across business and IT to deliver the API Products our business needs. We believe in creativity, courage, and smart investments to meet future customer demands.
   

  

 Who Are You?
   

  

 You are a product manager at heart, that can apply product management principles and thinking to products, processes or new technologies. You thrive in the intersection between business and IT, building trust across stakeholder groups. You are curious and not afraid to roll up your sleeves to drive change in a structured and result-oriented manner.
