title: Machine Learning Engineer
description:
**About us**


Within Digital Development, we drive digitalization and innovation for the Energy Distribution business. With data, analytics, and AI at the core, we build solutions that strengthen our processes, improve customer experience, and support a more sustainable energy system. The department consists of three teams – Data Analytics, Data Management, and Digital Services & Experience – working cross-functionally to develop and operate data and AI solutions and ensure high-quality data.


The Data Analytics team is our expert function within advanced analytics and AI, working closely with the business to identify opportunities, develop and deploy models, and enable data-driven decision-making. We are now strengthening the team with a Machine Learning Engineer who wants to work hands-on with data, technology, and real business impact – and grow with us.

**Your role**


As a Machine Learning Engineer, you will work at the intersection of data engineering and machine learning, building scalable solutions that enable data-driven decision-making across our business. A key part of the role is working with large volumes of smart meter data, ensuring it is processed, validated, and made available in a reliable and efficient way.


You will collaborate closely with data scientists, data engineers, and stakeholders to understand needs and turn them into practical solutions. The role combines hands-on development with continuous improvement of both solutions and ways of working.


In your role, you will:

* Build and maintain data pipelines for production
* Develop and deploy machine learning solutions and data products
* Work with large-scale datasets, primarily within smart metering
* Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business needs into technical solutions
* Continuously improve existing data products and workflows

We work according to DataOps and MLOps principles, with Data as a Product central to how we design, deliver, and operate our data and ML solutions.


**Who we are looking for**


We believe you have relevant experience, including building scalable data pipelines, preferably using PySpark and Databricks. A university degree in IT or a similar field is beneficial, but we value practical experience and curiosity just as much.


You also have experience in several of the following areas:

* Python and SQL
* Cloud platforms, preferably Azure
* CI/CD pipelines and Git-based version control
* Applying machine learning techniques in practice

Experience with DataOps and MLOps practices is considered a plus.


Beyond your technical skills, we place great value on how you work and collaborate. You are a curious and solution-oriented person who enjoys exploring new ideas and continuously improving how things are done. You take ownership of your work while collaborating closely with others, and feel comfortable navigating complexity, even when not all answers are given from the start.


Since the role involves close collaboration with both technical teams and business stakeholders, you are comfortable communicating and enjoy turning needs into practical solutions. You are a team player who values collaboration, contributes to an open and supportive environment, and believes that the best results are achieved together. Professional English is required, and Swedish is considered a plus.

**How is it to work with us?**


Our culture is characterized by curiosity, collaboration, and a shared ambition to create real impact. We believe in an open environment with high psychological safety, where we support each other, share knowledge, and grow together.


A warm welcome to apply and join us on the journey toward a sustainable society! The application deadline is April 10th.

**For questions about the position, please contact the hiring manager:**


Caroline Nyquist Alvarez, Caroline.NyquistAlvarez@eon.se

**For union-related questions, please contact:**


Unionen, Unionen.EED@eon.se  

Akademikerna, Akademikerna@eon.se  

Mattias Andolfsson, Ledarna, mattias.andolfsson@eon.se  

Mats Lundberg, SEKO, +46 730 499778

title: Technical Documentation Specialist/ Teknikinformatör
description:
Lindinvent utvecklar egen hårdvara och mjukvara för behovsstyrd ventilation och fastighetsautomation. Genom att kombinera sensorer, elektronik och intelligenta styrsystem skapas energieffektiva lösningar som optimerar inneklimat, belysning och solskydd i exempelvis kontor, skolor, laboratorier och vårdmiljöer. Målet är att skapa smarta fastigheter med hög komfort och minimal energianvändning.


Vi är en marknadsledande leverantör i teknikens framkant, med kunder bland Sveriges största fastighetsägare. Företaget befinner sig i en spännande expansionsfas och tar nästa steg genom flytt till nya lokaler.

**Om rollen**


Rollen inom teknisk dokumentation har till uppgift att säkerställa att företagets produkter och system beskrivs och dokumenteras på ett tydligt, korrekt och användbart sätt. Det handlar om att ta fram och förvalta teknisk produktinformation som gör det möjligt för projektorganisation, installatörer, driftpersonal och kunder att förstå hur produkterna fungerar, installeras och används.


I en organisation som Lindinvent, där både hårdvara, elektronik, mjukvara och styrsystem utvecklas internt, blir dokumentationen en central del av produktens funktion och användbarhet. Rollen bidrar därför direkt till att säkerställa att tekniken kan implementeras, driftsättas och användas korrekt i verkliga fastigheter.

**Arbetsuppgifter**


Du kommer att ansvara för att ta fram och utveckla teknisk produktinformation. Dokumentationen omfattar exempelvis produktbeskrivningar, tekniska datablad, manualer, driftsättnings- och injusteringsanvisningar, inkopplingsscheman samt installations- och användarinstruktioner. Arbetet innefattar även att utforma visuella delar av dokumentationen, såsom tekniska illustrationer, kopplingsscheman, symboler och grafiska strukturer, där både tydlighet och visuell kvalitet bidrar till att göra informationen lätt att förstå. Rollen ansvarar också för produktrelaterad märkning och identifiering, exempelvis produktetiketter, skyltar och annan teknisk märkning kopplad till produkterna och deras installationer. I takt med att informationsbehoven utvecklas kan rollen även bidra till nya former av teknisk information, såsom instruktionsfilmer, visuella guider eller andra digitala stöd för installation och driftsättning.

 **Arbetssätt idag**


Du arbetar nära utvecklingsavdelningen för att förstå produktens uppbyggnad och funktion, där underlaget kan bestå av konstruktionsdata, komponentinformation, scheman och tekniska specifikationer. Informationen kompletteras vid behov genom dialog med utvecklare, leverantörer och andra tekniska funktioner. Utifrån detta material tas en första version av dokumentationen fram, ofta baserad på etablerade mallar för exempelvis produktblad, manualer eller driftsättningsanvisningar. Dokumentationen utvecklas därefter successivt i takt med att produkterna används i projekt och erfarenheter från installation och drift återförs till materialet. Du fungerar därmed som en viktig länk mellan produktutveckling, projektorganisation och den praktiska användningen av systemen. Arbetet innebär samverkan med flera delar av organisationen, där utvecklingsavdelningen är en central partner, men även projekt- och försäljningsorganisationen bidrar med krav och behov kopplade till produktinformation i olika projekt. Det innebär därför att hantera flera interna kravställare och säkerställa att nödvändig information tas fram.


Idag används inget PIM-system i verksamheten. Införande av ett sådant system är ett prioriterat mål framåt och kommer att utgöra en del av rollen.

**Din kompetens och bakgrund**


Rollen passar en person med erfarenhet av teknisk dokumentation eller teknisk informationsproduktion, exempelvis som teknikinformatör, teknisk skribent eller teknisk dokumentatör. Erfarenhet från tekniska miljöer där produkter utvecklas och implementeras är särskilt värdefull, till exempel inom ventilation, fastighetsautomation, elektronik eller andra system där hårdvara, styrsystem och mjukvara samverkar. En erfarenhetsnivå på cirka 5 till 10 år inom teknisk dokumentation eller teknisk kommunikation är tillräcklig. Har du dessutom en fallenhet för grafisk design och ett öga för hur information presenteras visuellt är det en styrka.


En lämplig utbildningsbakgrund kan vara inom teknisk kommunikation, informationsdesign, teknisk dokumentation eller annan teknisk eller ingenjörsinriktad utbildning där informationsstruktur ingår. Samtidigt är praktisk erfarenhet och dokumenterad förmåga att arbeta med teknisk information viktigare än en specifik utbildning. Rollen kräver ett genuint tekniskt intresse, analytisk förmåga och ett strukturerat arbetssätt samt en pedagogisk förmåga att förklara tekniska funktioner på ett begripligt sätt. Arbetet innebär ofta att själv driva informationsinhämtning och utveckla dokumentation i samverkan med flera delar av organisationen, vilket gör initiativförmåga och självständighet viktiga egenskaper.


God vana vid verktyg för teknisk dokumentation och grafisk produktion är en förutsättning. Adobe Creative Cloud är centralt i arbetet, särskilt InDesign för layout och produktion av manualer och tekniska dokument, Illustrator för tekniska illustrationer och scheman, Photoshop för bildbearbetning samt Acrobat för hantering och kvalitetssäkring av PDF-dokument. Premiere Pro kan användas för att producera enklare instruktions- och driftsättningsfilmer. Rollen kommer även att arbeta med digitala verktyg för hantering av produktinformation, exempelvis PIM-lösningar eller andra system för strukturerad produktdata. Svenska är huvudspråket i dokumentationen, men eftersom mycket teknisk information även finns på engelska krävs god förmåga att arbeta på båda språken. Ytterligare språkkunskaper, exempelvis norska eller danska, är meriterande men inte avgörande.

**Framtida utmaningar och utvecklingsområden**


Företaget befinner sig i en utvecklingsfas där både produktportfölj och informationsbehov växer. Produkterna blir mer avancerade och integrerar fler tekniska komponenter såsom sensorer, styrsystem och digitala gränssnitt, vilket innebär att mängden teknisk dokumentation kommer att öka. Samtidigt behöver informationen bli mer strukturerad och tillgänglig för olika målgrupper och marknader, vilket ställer krav på utvecklade arbetssätt för hur teknisk information tas fram, organiseras och förvaltas.


Ett viktigt utvecklingsområde är hantering av produktinformation i digitala system, exempelvis PIM-lösningar, där teknisk information kan struktureras och återanvändas i flera olika kanaler. Parallellt finns ett växande behov av mer genomarbetade manualer och driftsättningsanvisningar, särskilt eftersom systemen installeras i komplexa miljöer såsom skolor, kontor och laboratorier där ventilation och klimatstyrning är kritiska funktioner. Samtidigt utvecklas arbetet kring hållbarhet och miljörelaterad produktinformation, exempelvis miljödata och produktdeklarationer, vilket innebär att den tekniska dokumentationen i ökande grad behöver samverka med dessa informationsflöden.

title: Fullstack Engineer
description:
### **What we do at Detectify**

  

We’re Detectify, and we’re not your average cybersecurity company. We’re a team of passionate engineers and hackers who are obsessed with building cutting-edge tech that actually makes the internet a safer place to be. We’re talking about real-life hackers who know their stuff. You’ll learn from the best, and maybe even develop some elite skills yourself.  

  

Detectify was founded by ethical hackers in 2013, and offers an application security platform trusted by over 2,100 organizations globally, from high-growth startups to the world’s largest enterprises and public institutions. We equip modern security teams with clarity and control over their attack surface. Fueled by real-world research from our global community of elite ethical hackers and scaled through our own AI-driven engines, we enable organizations to identify and fix truly exploitable vulnerabilities before attackers do. We believe that the fear of cyber threats should never stand in the way of digital greatness.

### **What you'll get to do**


You will play a significant part in evolving our product to the next level. We try to build services that not only solve today’s problems but also enable future solutions already today. We will ask you to think broader than just the task at hand and hopefully you thrive in this environment.


We strive to create an enabling and empowered culture. Here you will have the ability to make use of all your skills and personality. A fun and engaging environment where we can have unfiltered and passionate discussions to learn and grow together. As an engineer in a cross-functional product team you will get to think it, build it, ship it, measure it, own it, tweak it, and as part of that:

* Team up with brilliant engineers, designers, and hackers to build the next generation of cybersecurity tools.
* Dive deep into event-based microservices, serverless architecture, and all things AWS.
* Get your hands dirty across the full tech-stack
* Together with your team, own your products from innovation to production and see the direct impact of your work.
* Experiment with the latest AI tools and help us use them responsibly to move ever faster

#### **Technologies you will get your hands on:**

* Event-based microservices and serverless architecture running on AWS
* Modern development tools across the entire stack: Golang, Kafka, Terraform, AWS ECS/Lambda/AppSync, GraphQL, React, Typescript
* Claude Code as AI-Assistance tool to unleash your productivity

### **Skills & Requirements**

#### **Who you are**

* You're a fullstack engineer with a growing passion for cybersecurity
* You thrive in a collaborative environment and love solving complex challenges with your team
* You're not afraid to ask "why" and challenge the status quo
* You're eager to learn, grow, and make a real difference in the world of cybersecurity.
* You're passionate about customer and business needs and one of your drivers is to deliver value quickly to continuously iterate on even better and fuller solutions.
* You typically come from a background where you are used to working with SaaS products using microservices at scale in a Cloud-First environment
* You don’t use AI tools to “vibe code” but use it as an assistant to amplify your skills

#### **So, what do you think?**


We are proud to foster an inclusive workplace free from discrimination. We strongly believe that diversity of experience, perspectives, and background leads to a better environment and a better product. We encourage everyone to be a part of changing the way the world thinks about security!

#### **Hybrid work:**


This position is for our office in central Stockholm. We offer hybrid working and would like to see you a few days per week

**Go hack yourself!**

title: Fullstack Engineer - Billing Platform
description:
**Introducing evroc**


At evroc, we are building a secure, sovereign, and sustainable hyperscale cloud to reimagine the digital future of Europe. By joining our company, you have an exciting opportunity to contribute to the development of next-generation cloud services. We are seeking intellectually curious and highly motivated team members who are ready to embrace the thrilling challenge of building the first European owned hyperscale cloud.

**Location:** Stockholm

**Job type:** Permanent position

**Join the Pioneers**


We’re looking for a Software Engineer to build evroc’s next-generation Billing Platform, a system that processes millions of events and powers usage-based pricing, invoicing, and revenue flows across our cloud.


In this role, you will work across the full stack, connecting event-driven backend systems with intuitive, data-heavy user interfaces. You’ll build the tools that people rely on to understand and manage complex billing scenarios with accuracy and confidence.


This is a high-impact role where you’ll own features end-to-end and help define how billing is experienced across evroc’s platform.

**What You’ll Tackle**

* Design and build fullstack features across the billing lifecycle, from backend services to user-facing interfaces.
* Develop billing consoles and dashboards, including usage visualisation, invoice management, and subscription workflows.
* Build tools for managing pricing models, SKUs, and discount structures used by product and commercial teams.
* Design APIs that abstract complex billing logic into clear and reliable contracts for frontend consumption.
* Implement data-heavy interfaces such as cost projections, usage trends, and reconciliation views.
* Establish frontend patterns and components for scalable, high-performance administrative interfaces.
* Work closely with product and design to translate complex billing concepts into intuitive user workflows.
* Integrate billing systems with external services such as payment providers, accounting systems, and CRMs.

**Skills We Value**

* 5+ years of experience in fullstack engineering, ideally building data-intensive web applications.
* Backend experience in Golang or Python, building APIs and handling authentication, versioning, and middleware.
* Strong proficiency in React and TypeScript
* Solid understanding of databases (SQL/NoSQL), including schema design, indexing, and performance considerations.
* Experience working with complex system integrations and external services.
* Familiarity with SaaS billing concepts such as invoicing, usage-based pricing, and subscription management.
* Ability to translate complex data and systems into clear, reliable user experiences.

**Why Join evroc?**


We offer a competitive salary and an equity package to attract the best. Whether it’s the cosmopolitan allure of London, the vibrant Stockholm, or the Mediterranean charm of Sophia Antipolis, you’re poised for an inspiring work environment and a captivating local culture!


At evroc, diversity is our strength. We champion an inclusive environment where every background - ethnicity, age, gender identity, beliefs, and culture - is celebrated.


Applicants must possess a valid work permit.

title: PO/SM Cloud Governance & Security
description:
**Let’s shape the future together**  

We’re shaping our business around people. While our cars embrace leading-edge technologies, we never employ technology for its own sake. Quite the opposite. Instead, our focus is on adapting technology to benefit people. This philosophy extends to the sales and customer experience we aim to deliver. Do you want to be a part of creating the exciting solutions of tomorrow’s mobility? Then look no further, join us.

**Let's introduce ourselves**
-----------------------------



The Cloud Governance & Security Product Owner is responsible for defining, driving, and maintaining Volvo Cars cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, GCP). The role acts as the strategic bridge between security, compliance, operations, and development teams—ensuring that cloud environments remain secure, compliant, cost‑efficient, and aligned with enterprise strategies.

  



This position owns the product backlog for cloud governance and security capabilities, continuously improving the controls, automation, and guardrails that enable secure-by-design cloud adoption across the organization.

**What you'll do**
------------------

  

**Product ownership and strategy**

  


* Own cloud governance and security product vision, roadmap, and backlog.
* Convert business and regulatory needs into OKRs and delivery-ready backlog items.
* Track KPIs for security posture, governance controls, cloud spend, and adoption.
* Prioritize work by risk, value, compliance, and operational impact.

  


**Product leadership**

  


* Align direction with architecture, security leadership, and business stakeholders.
* Drive cross-cloud prioritization and follow-up with delivery teams, removing blockers.
* Ensure enterprise-grade security, scalability, resilience, and compliance in delivered features.
* Strengthen one-team ways of working and continuous improvement.

  


**Security governance and risk management**

  


* Co-lead cloud and identity governance with Cybersecurity and Cloud Solution Architecture.
* Maintain alignment to Zero Trust, NIST, CIS, and ISO 27002.
* Own the cloud governance and security framework (policies, standards, tagging, cost governance, lifecycle, CNAPP).
* Improve landing zones, IAM lifecycle, vulnerability management, and policy enforcement using a risk-based approach.
* Enable automation-first and self-service capabilities.
* Support audits and manage cloud risk identification and mitigation.

  


**Stakeholder and executive engagement**

  


* Act as executive point of contact for strategy, progress, and risks.
* Communicate roadmap, OKRs, and releases with clear, data-driven updates.
* Partner across platform, engineering, operations, compliance, and vendors to drive adoption.

  


**Lifecycle and performance management**

  


* Define and track success metrics (risk reduction, SLAs and SLOs, posture, experience).
* Use threat, incident, usage, and cost insights to optimize the ecosystem.
* Support secure deprecation and migration from legacy platforms to modern alternatives.

  





**What you'll bring**
---------------------

  

**Technical and domain expertise**

  


* Strong cloud security and governance background across Azure, AWS, or GCP, including landing zones and security architecture.
* Deep IAM knowledge: SSO, MFA, federation, conditional access, identity lifecycle, RBAC or ABAC, and privileged access.
* Practical experience with security controls for network, workloads, data, monitoring, and automation.
* Familiar with key frameworks: Zero Trust, NIST CSF, CIS Controls, ISO 27002, and enterprise IAM models.
* Clear grasp of confidentiality, integrity, and availability principles.
* Strong stakeholder management and communication.

  


**Product and leadership skills**

  


* 3+ years in senior product or security leadership roles in enterprise environments.
* Experience leading strategic platforms or transformation initiatives.
* Ability to translate security requirements into a clear product strategy and roadmap.
* Communicates effectively with executives, stakeholders, and technical teams.

  


**Preferred qualifications (beneficial but not mandatory)**

  


* Cloud certifications such as Azure Security Engineer, Azure Solutions Architect, AWS Security Specialty, and similar.
* Background in cybersecurity, risk management, and cloud platform and architecture management.
 **Volvo Cars. For Life.**  

For nearly a century, Volvo Cars has empowered people to move freely in a personal, sustainable and safe way. Today, we are driving bold advancements in electrification, sustainability and automotive safety. To realise our ambitious vision, we are seeking innovative minds who are ready to tackle the challenges of tomorrow – today.  

  

In our company, we believe extraordinary things are achieved by ordinary people with the drive to make a difference.
  

  

**Ready to take the next step?**  

Submit your CV in English and tell us why you’re the ideal candidate for a new role at Volvo Cars. Applications must be received no later than 12th of April 2026. We are prioritising direct applications to ensure a fair and efficient application process.  

  

As part of the recruitment process, the final candidates might undergo a background check.  

  

We welcome your application!

title: Director, Enterprise
description:
Do you want to help solve the world's toughest business problems with Data and AI? This is what we do every day at Databricks.
   

  

 We are looking for an Enterprise Sales Director to join our growing business in the Nordics Leading a team of Enterprise Account Executives, you will be measured by achieving your team's overall quota and growing Databricks consumption. This is a team of account executives who are passionate about building a data ecosystem in the Nordic Region, technically knowledgeable and have a strong desire to drive value for our customers.
   

  

 This is a fantastic opportunity to build upon a highly successful team achieving significant year on year growth, in a fast growing business which is a hugely impactful part of the EMEA sales organisation. We are looking for owners, who will go the extra mile and want to be the very best at what they do.
   

  

 The position will report to the RVP, Enterprise for the Nordics.
   

  

**The Impact You Will Have**
* Lead a team of account executives, ensuring you coach them to develop the skills and behaviours they will need to succeed
* Establish company territory plans, team structure, individual quotas and patches for your team, including investment capacity requirements
* Report on revenue forecast and strategic GTM programs
* Partner with cross-functional teams to manage a complete revenue and customer success process
* Inspire a culture of teamwork, leading with value and achieving desired customers outcomes
* Develop trust-based relationships with customers and partners to ensure long-term success
* Encourage learning and ongoing understanding of technical product details and our future product roadmap
* Establish a revenue growth and investment plan in the first 90 days
* Deliver our strategic growth plans, in collaboration with the other function leaders across EMEA, ensure forecast accuracy and a predictable, high-growth business


**What We Look For**
* Experience as a high-growth enterprise software sales leader with experience leading sales teams in the Nordics within the Data, AI, Cloud, or SaaS Sales Industry
* History of exceeding sales quotas in similar high-growth technology companies
* Ability to engage with and hire sales talent in the market
* Focus and emphasis on methodology-based sales coaching, MEDDPIC and a Challenger mentality
* Experience of value-based sales with both the business and IT stakeholders including C suite
* Experience in leadership roles focused on managing sales organisations to influence, develop, and achieve objectives within Data, AI, Cloud, or SaaS sales
* Extended Executive relationship network with key customers
* Knowledge of the partner ecosystem to help grow Enterprise strategic territories
* Success implementing strategies for consumption and commitment-based sales revenue models
* Business travel is also required outside Sweden


**About Databricks**
 Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on Twitter, LinkedIn and Facebook.
   

  

**Benefits**
 At Databricks, we strive to provide comprehensive benefits and perks that meet the needs of all of our employees. For specific details on the benefits offered in your region, please visit https://www.mybenefitsnow.com/databricks.
   

  

**Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion**
 At Databricks, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone can excel. We take great care to ensure that our hiring practices are inclusive and meet equal employment opportunity standards. Individuals looking for employment at Databricks are considered without regard to age, color, disability, ethnicity, family or marital status, gender identity or expression, language, national origin, physical and mental ability, political affiliation, race, religion, sexual orientation, socio-economic status, veteran status, and other protected characteristics.
   

  

**Compliance**
**If access to export-controlled technology or source code is required for performance of job duties, it is within Employer's discretion whether to apply for a U.S. government license for such positions, and Employer may decline to proceed with an applicant on this basis alone.**

title: Technology Management Consultant
description:
The key word is change. In a world where technology and business are merging, we are seeking a Technology Management Consultant to plan, lead, and execute IT projects and transformation initiatives, taking ownership of delivery from scoping through to implementation across business applications and technical platforms.
   

  

**Who is Umain?**
 We were founded with a
 **heritage in digital product development and engineering** 
 . This background gives us a unique perspective, ensuring that our strategic advice is always grounded in
 **technical feasibility and execution excellence** 
 . We are now expanding our focus to help clients at the C-level, linking core technology strategy directly to business outcomes.
   

  

 Umain is proudly part of
 **Eidra** 
 , a consultancy collective driven by the conviction that business transformation requires a combination of management consulting and best-in-breed expertise in creativity, engineering, and data. This partnership means that while you focus on strategic IT-Management and enterprise architecture, you have full access to orchestrate common capabilities from the best specialized companies within the collective.
   

  

 Our team consists of individuals with experience from the largest consulting firms, but with the flexibility, speed, and entrepreneurial drive that characterize a modern, flat organization. At Umain, we highly value
 **collaboration** 
 and
 **entrepreneurial spirit** 
 . Here, you are not only expected to lead demanding client assignments; you are also expected to actively contribute to our internal development, whether it involves shaping new service offerings in AI/Data or mentoring our junior talent. We reward those who take ownership and drive us forward.
   

  

**The Role: What will you do?**
 As a Technology Management Consultant Within Tech Advisory & IT Management, You Work At The Intersection Of Business And Technology, Helping Clients Structure And Execute Their IT Initiatives. Your Assignments Will Involve
   

  

* IT Strategy & Enterprise Architecture: Supporting clients in technology direction, investment priorities, and portfolio decisions. Helping translate business goals into future-state architecture for business-critical applications.
* Governance, Operating Model & Sourcing: Helping clients design the structures that make technology delivery work, from IT governance frameworks and risk management to operating model design, vendor strategy, and FinOps.
* Project & Program Management: Leading and coordinating IT projects and workstreams, managing teams, tracking delivery, and ensuring strategic intent translates into execution reality.
* Internal Contribution: Actively contributing to Eidra's business development, mentoring junior colleagues, and developing our competency pool.


**Who are you?**
**We Are Looking For Someone Who**
* Has 3+ years of relevant experience, preferably within a consultancy setting, across IT project management or technology transformation, in roles such as IT Consultant, Project Manager, Business Analyst, or Solution Architect.
* Is driven by a passion for translating technology strategy into measurable business outcomes.
* Has experience leading and coordinating IT initiatives and cross-functional project teams.
* Is a collaborative team player who creates structure, clarity, and momentum in delivery.
* Communicates complex technical concepts clearly to both technical teams and business stakeholders.


**Required Competencies**
* IT Domain Knowledge: Working knowledge across at least two of Eidra's five IT management domains: IT Strategy & Portfolio, Enterprise Architecture & Data, Operating Model & Workforce, Governance Risk & Trust, or Sourcing & FinOps.
* System & Architecture Experience: Hands-on experience with enterprise platforms and business-critical systems (e.g. ERP, CRM, modern data stacks, proprietary solutions), combined with a foundational understanding of integration patterns and cloud architecture.
* Strategic AI: A solid understanding of how to utilize and integrate AI tools and technologies in core business processes and systems.
* Collaborative Mindset: A collaborative and open approach, with a genuine commitment to continuous development, team building, and long-term client relationships.
* Analytical Drive: A critical-thinking mindset with the ability to identify bottlenecks and opportunities, and translate complexity into clear, actionable recommendations.
* Language: Fluency in English, both written and spoken, is essential.


***Background check***
*Umain conducts a background check for final candidates in accordance with our* 
 internal policies
 *. The check is carried out only with your consent and is ordered by our CISO through our approved screening partner. The scope of the check depends on the role level. This step is performed late in the recruitment process and is used solely to support a secure and fact‑based hiring decision.*
 Umain is a digital product consultancy. We shape, ship and scale digital products at speed. Founded in Stockholm in 2001, we are a growing team of 100+ experts in digital product strategy, design, and technology.
   

  

 We are a part of Eidra, a group of top-notch companies specializing in strategy, creativity, and engineering. As a collective we guide leaders in making meaningful and lasting change.

title: Account Executive
description:
**TL;DR** 





 We are looking for a business-driven Account Executive who knows how to win complex B2B deals, build long-term customer relationships, and help shape a world-class go-to-market motion. You will sell an advanced AI platform to mid-market and enterprise companies, primarily in the Nordic markets, and play a key role in Agaton’s next growth phase.
 




  





**Ready to Shape the Future of B2B Sales?** 




  





**Join Agaton, where advanced AI transforms how sales and customer service teams perform.** 




**We are not building yet another analytics tool. We are building the intelligence layer that helps companies understand conversations, coach people, automate workflows, and fundamentally improve how they sell and serve customers.** 




  






 If you thrive in complex B2B sales, enjoy working closely with product and founders, and want to be part of building a category-defining company from Europe, this role is for you.
 




  





**The Opportunity** 





 As a Account Executive at Agaton, you will own and drive high-impact sales processes with mid-market and enterprise customers across the Nordic region. You will work closely with founders, product, and engineering to translate real customer problems into clear value propositions and long-term partnerships.
 





 This is not a “run inbound and close quick” role. You will sell transformational software with multiple stakeholders, varied sales cycles, and real strategic impact for our customers.
 




  





**What You’ll Be Doing** 



* Own complex sales cycles from first conversation to signed agreement
* Engage with senior decision makers including Heads of Sales, Customer Service, Operations, CX, and Revenue
* Build and manage strong relationships with key customers and partners in the Scandinavian markets
* Lead discovery to deeply understand customer pain points and business priorities
* Position Agaton’s AI platform as a strategic solution, not just a tool
* Work closely with Product and Engineering to tailor demos, pilots, and POCs to customer needs
* Collaborate with marketing and leadership to continuously refine messaging, ICPs, and go-to-market strategy
* Contribute directly to shaping our sales playbook as we scale internationally



**What We’re Looking For** 



* Proven experience in senior B2B sales, preferably within software, SaaS, or data-driven platforms
* Strong track record of closing complex, high-value deals across mid-market and enterprise segments
* Experience selling to companies in the Nordic or broader Scandinavian markets
* Ability to navigate multi-stakeholder buying processes with confidence and structure
* Strong commercial mindset combined with genuine curiosity about technology and AI
* Comfortable working in a fast-moving, founder-led growth environment
* Ability to remain calm, structured, and credible in high-pressure sales situations
* Willingness to travel as part of the role
* Fluent in both Swedish and English, spoken and written



  





**Bonus Points** 



* Experience selling AI, analytics, voice, CX, or sales technology
* Background in telecom, financial services, utilities, or other complex verticals
* Experience working in or with scaleups or high-growth startups
* Familiarity with procurement, security reviews, and legal processes
* Experience working closely with product or technical teams during sales cycles



  





**What We Offer** 



* Competitive salary with an attractive commission and incentive structure
* Above-market stock option package
* Six weeks annual paid vacation
* Wellness budget to invest in your health and wellbeing
* Learning and development budget
* Stockholm-based role with flexibility and a modern office environment
* A rare opportunity to sell a truly differentiated product with real market traction
* Direct impact on how a category-defining company grows and scales



  





**About Agaton** 





 At Agaton, we're building the intelligence layer behind every customer conversation, going beyond transcripts to understand sales at a granular level: not just what's said, but how it's said. We analyze language, silence, and vocal tone to coach human agents in real time and power autonomous AI voice agents that compound in performance the more they're used.
 





 Our technology understands context, adapts in real time, and helps companies move from reactive customer interactions to intelligent, value-driven communication. We believe sales should be helpful, intelligent, and genuinely valuable, not pushy or scripted.
 





 We are a high-growth, talent-dense team based in Stockholm, backed by leading angels and operators from companies like Spotify, Klarna, Shopify, Sana, and more. We are expanding rapidly across Europe and preparing for global scale.
 




  





**Ready to Drive the Future?** 





 We are looking for experienced B2B sellers who want more than just a quota. If you want to help define a category, work closely with founders, and sell technology that genuinely changes how companies operate, we would love to hear from you.
 




  






 This is more than a job. It is a chance to be part of building something generation-defining.
 




  





**Apply now. You only need to submit your CV. No cover letter required.** 




  






 This position is based in Stockholm, Sweden. Please submit your application in English, as it is our company language.

title: Assistant Vice President – Technology Risk Management
description:
Are you an established leader within a risk function with experience managing global teams? Do you want to actively contribute and deliver to the team's success as well leading and developing the team - Then this role might be your next step!
   

  

 The Assistant Vice President (AVP), Technology Risk Management is a senior leadership role responsible for defining and delivering technology risk strategy across Nasdaq’s regulated market infrastructure and technology solutions businesses. The role combines deep business knowledge of the exchanges industry with applied technology, cyber, and operational risk expertise, ensuring technology risks are managed in line with regulatory expectations, business priorities, and Nasdaq’s risk appetite.
   

  

 The AVP will lead a globally distributed team, engage directly with senior executives, Boards, and regulators, and represent Technology Risk Management in internal forums. This is a hands‑on leadership role, requiring both strategic direction and active involvement in delivery.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

* Define and implement technology risk strategy aligned to Nasdaq’s Enterprise Risk Management framework and risk appetite
* Apply Financial Market Infrastructure (FMI) and market‑structure knowledge to assess technology risk across the full trading lifecycle and supporting market services
* Lead all aspects of technology risk coverage across regulated exchanges and critical infrastructure, as well as technology solutions businesses including data, analytics, SaaS, cloud, and digital platforms
* Provide senior oversight and challenge across topics including cyber security, cloud, third‑party risk, operational resilience, and technology change
* Support regulatory examinations and ongoing engagement with U.S. and European regulators as required (e.g. SEC, CFTC, ESMA, European national regulators)
* Prepare and present technology risk insights to Boards, executive committees, and senior governance forums
* Drive risk management initiatives for emerging technologies, including AI/ML, digital assets, quantum and cloud adoption
* Lead, coordinate, and develop a globally distributed technology risk team, setting clear priorities and standards
* Partner with and challenge the 1st line operations teams as required to enhance the controls environment
* Collaborate with the broader Group Risk Team, Legal, Compliance, InfoSec and Internal Audit teams to maximise the value of combined assurance across 2nd and 3rd line teams


**Required Qualifications**
* Extensive validated experience in technology risk, operational risk, cyber risk, or technology leadership within financial services, exchanges / market infrastructure
* Confirmed ability to pair business knowledge of the exchanges industry with technology risk management concepts
* Experience supporting or overseeing regulated market infrastructure alongside unregulated or commercial technology businesses
* Proven experience presenting to Boards, senior management, and regulators in the U.S. and Europe
* Experience managing risk across a portfolio of businesses with varied risk appetites


**Technical and Risk Expertise**
* Strong knowledge of cyber security, operational resilience, cloud, third‑party risk
* Understanding of critical market infrastructure requirements, including availability, recovery, capacity, and incident management
* Experience governing risks related to AI, digital assets, and cloud technologies


 If you have any relevant certifications or qualifications in Technology Risk, Operational Risk, Cyber Security, AI, Digital Risk, or Cloud (e.g. CISSP, CISM, or equivalent), please state those in your resume.
   

  

 This position is located in Stockholm, Sweden, and offers a hybrid work environment with at least 3 days per week in the office.
   

  

**About Nasdaq Stockholm**
 Our new Nasdaq Stockholm office is located at Mäster Samuelsgatan 56, right in the heart of the city. The building is easily accessible by public transport—just steps from T-centralen and several major bus stops. The workspace is designed for flexibility and collaboration, with open neighborhoods that support both focused work and teamwork.
   

  

 Nasdaq is a vibrant and entrepreneurial company where everyone is encouraged to take initiative, challenge the status quo, and take intelligent risks. We want everyone to feel welcome and bring their authentic self to work. Every day, we build a culture where we all feel connected, supported, and empowered.
   

  

**What We Offer**
* Competitive base salary
* Annual bonus
* Annual equity grant
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan offering discounted company shares
* Pension plan
* 32 paid vacation days
* 6 additional days off per year
* Fitness Allowance/Friskvårdsbidrag
* Work from (almost) anywhere – up to 20 days/year
* Paid time off to volunteer
* Health insurance
* 24/7 mental health support for you and your family
* Access to e-learning platforms


 Learn more on our Nasdaq Benefits & Rewards Career Page.
   

  

**Come as You Are**
 Nasdaq is an equal opportunity employer. We positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of age, color, disability, national origin, ancestry, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by applicable law.
   

  

 We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request an accommodation.

title: Digital Workplace/Intranet Solution Architect
description:
We are currently looking for a Senior Solution Architect to join a large-scale intranet transformation program, focused on building a modern, scalable, and personalized digital workplace using Staffbase.
 



  





 In this role, you will define the end-to-end solution architecture, ensuring seamless integration with the enterprise IT landscape while meeting high standards for security, scalability, and performance.
 



  





 Contract Duration - 8 Months + Extensions
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




 • Define and own solution architecture for a unified digital workplace platform
 



 • Translate business and UX requirements into technical designs
 



 • Design integration patterns and identity models (SSO, federation)
 



 • Ensure compliance with security and enterprise architecture standards
 



 • Guide vendors and internal teams throughout delivery and implementation
 



 • Support migration from legacy intranet and SharePoint platforms
 



 • Contribute to technical roadmap and long-term platform strategy
 



  





**Key Requirements** 




 • Strong experience as a Solution Architect in enterprise environments
 



 • Experience with SaaS platforms and digital workplace/intranet implementations
 



 • Experience with SharePoint migrations or intranet consolidation projects
 



 • Strong knowledge of integration patterns (APIs, middleware)
 



 • Experience with identity and access management (Azure AD, SAML, OAuth, SSO)
 



 • Strong stakeholder management skills across business and IT
 



  





**Nice to Have** 




 • Experience with
 **Staffbase** 




 • Knowledge of
 **information architecture and content governance** 




 • Experience in
 **large global or industrial organization** 




  





 Interested? Apply Now

description:
