title: Application Support Engineer - (PCB/Data Management) - f/m/d
description:
### **About Us**


Siemens EDA is a global technology leader in Electronic Design Automation software. Our software tools enable companies around the world to develop new and highly innovative electronic products faster and more cost-effectively. Our customers use our tools to push the boundaries of technology and physics in order to deliver better products in the increasingly complex world of chip, board and system design.

### **Position Overview**


The Siemens EDA Global Support team is seeking a dynamic, Support Application Engineer for the Xpedition Enterprise tools suite with emphasis on EDM Server and data management. Building on EDA experience and system administration skills, this individual will serve as a key member of an award-winning Electronic Design Automation technical support team committed to providing excellent technical support. They will work directly with internal and external customers to resolve tool issues, partner with account teams to educate users and grow product usage and create knowledge content for the web-based support portal.

### **Responsibilities**

* Proactively assist customers with Xpedition EDM Server and related tools.
* Provide support via phone, electronically, or on-site to help customers meet project deadlines.
* Guide customers on implementing and maintaining Xpedition EDM Server and design flow tools.
* Collaborate with account teams to help struggling customers overcome challenges.
* Act as a liaison between customers and Electronic Board Systems engineering, providing feedback both ways.
* Participate regularly in customer meetings and conference calls (internal and external).
* Develop and communicate solutions for customer issues as part of the account team.
* Create knowledge base content (articles, white papers, videos) for web-based support.

### **Requirements**

* System administration experience.
* Broad knowledge of basic networking technology (LDAP/Active Directory, SSL, Kerberos).
* Experience with performance monitoring and virtualization.
* Proficiency in low-level application troubleshooting.
* Familiarity with FlexLM Licensing.
* Scripting experience (Linux shell, Windows batch file, PowerShell, or similar).
* Experience with Oracle or PostgreSQL based data systems.
* Self-motivated, collaborative, and able to thrive in a fast-paced environment.
* Experience with the administration of PCB design tools such as Xpedition Enterprise, Cadence Allegro, Altium or similar (desirable)
* Detailed knowledge of the ECAD Library development and design data management. Siemens Xpedition Enterprise EDM preferred (desirable)
* Familiarity with scripting languages such as Python and C# (desirable)

**Why us?**


Working at Siemens Software means flexibility - Choosing between working at home and the office at other times is the norm here.


We offer great benefits and rewards, as you'd expect from a world leader in industrial software. A collection of over 377,000 minds building the future, one day at a time in over 200 countries. We're dedicated to equality, and we welcome applications that reflect the diversity of the communities we work in. All employment decisions at Siemens are based on qualifications, merit, and business need.

**Bring your curiosity and creativity and help us shape tomorrow!**

**Siemens Software.**

**Transform the Everyday.**

**#LI-JR1**

**#LI-EDA**

**#LI-HYBRID**

title: Automotive Engineers | Stockholm
description:
Are you passionate about technology, electrification and product development within the automotive industry? At Together Tech, we are seeing strong demand across several R&D assignments within electrified powertrain and HV inverter development.
  
We are now looking to connect with driven engineers who want to work close to the technology and contribute to long‑term, high‑impact automotive projects. We work with leading players across passenger cars, heavy‑duty vehicles and e‑mobility, covering the full development cycle of electric drive systems, power electronics and related domains. Based on your technical background and career ambitions, we will match you with assignments where your competence makes an impact — while supporting your long‑term professional growth. We are currently looking for engineers within several engineering disciplines related to HV inverter and electric powertrain development, including:
  
**Mechanical Engineering:** Mechanical design of automotive inverters, including detailed component design, 3D modelling and drawing creation in SolidWorks. Responsibilities include design verification, requirement management, test cases and test reports, working closely with electronics, systems and test engineers.
  
**Test Engineering:** contribute to system‑ and product‑level testing within automotive development, including electrical and environmental testing, test specification development, execution and reporting, using laboratory equipment such as oscilloscopes, power meters and climate chambers and test automation with Robot Framework(Python) and LabVIEW.
  
* Hardware / Power Electronics Engineering: Contribute to LV/HV automotive inverter development, including hardware design and verification, power stage and auxiliary circuit design, schematic capture in Cadence, simulation in LTspice, lab testing, troubleshooting and EMC‑aware documentation. Application Engineer / SW Design Engineer: act as a technical interface towards customers, combining customer support and product configuration with embedded software development and and troubleshooting, using C programming and Robot Framework(Python) together with tools such as Jenkins and Jira.


Your technical bakgrund
  
  

A Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field with several years of experience in the automotive industry.
  
  

**You have experience in one or more of the following areas:** mechanical design within automotive applications, electrical or environmental testing and verification, embedded software development, or hardware and power electronics development.
  
  

Good command of written and spoken English, proficiency in Swedish is considered an advantage.
  
  

Your personal characteristics Being a consultant puts extra light on having a flexible and curious mindset, a self-reliant way of working and your capability to build strong relationships with customers and colleagues, while keeping track of the target picture. You stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies and are motivated to develop together with us.
  
Together Tech for sustainability We provide opportunities for development through a variety of assignments in different industries, whether it be client projects or in one of our exciting in-house projects. At Together Tech, you work together with other engineers who are passionate about the environment and sustainability, all with a desire to create lasting solutions using technology - or as we say: Better World Tech!
  
Your development & our support Your development and well-being are important to us, and Together Tech offers benefits that support you and your career in the best way. We value the importance of secure employment and fixed salary, and we have a collective agreement. We prioritize your health and provide a wellness allowance of 4000 SEK per year. Moreover, we offer health check-ups, contributions to activities, and wellness discounts.
  
We enjoy spending time together through various social activities and team-building activities suitable for everyone! From after-works and sport activities to pentathlons and corporate conferences. Our commitment is to give you great conditions to grow through various assignments and projects as well as individual development plans. We also offer courses in sustainability and other courses within the framework of your role and goals. We take pride in being recognized as one of Sweden's most attractive employers in 2026, 2025 and 2024 by Karriärföretagen!
  
Learn more about us at www.togethertech.com.
  
For questions regarding the position, please contact Business Unit Manager Anna Öfwerman on +46790 738 026

title: QA engineer
description:
Are you passionate about software quality and excited to test products that combine AI, computer vision, and hardware in real-world environments?
  
Do you thrive in a flexible, fast-paced startup setting, where your testing directly shapes the product and every day brings a new challenge?
  
  

**Who we are:**  

Blue Cromos AB is a Swedish software company pioneering brand protection through patented and innovative SaaS solutions that integrate cutting-edge AI and classic computer vision. Our platform empowers industries like manufacturing, logistics, pharma, luxury goods and automotive, delivering seamless quality assurance from the factory floor to the end user. We are a diverse, close-knit team of about 15, including management, engineers, architects, and testers, united by creativity, technical excellence, and a commitment to solving real client problems.
  
  

**What you’ll do:**  

As a QA Engineer, you will take a key role in strengthening our quality assurance practices across mobile, web, and data-driven systems. You will collaborate closely with developers, ML/CV engineers, and product stakeholders to plan and execute testing activities, improve our QA strategy, and help build a more systematic approach to quality.
  
  

**Your responsibilities will include:**  

Owning end-to-end testing of our mobile app and web dashboards, from exploratory testing to structured test cases.
  
Designing, maintaining, and running automated tests (e.g. with pytest, BrowserStack or similar tools).
  
Creating and maintaining test plans, test cases, and regression suites in a test management tool.
  
Logging, prioritizing, and following up on issues together with developers in our issue tracking system.
  
Testing data-driven and AI-supported features, validating datasets and checking system behavior across different scenarios and environments.
  
Performing exploratory testing, identifying edge cases, and challenging assumptions to uncover risks early.
  
**Contributing to QA processes and strategy:** helping us improve how we plan testing, structure releases, and connect QA with development and R&D.
  
Collaborating on performance and reliability checks, including testing on different devices, OS versions, and network conditions.
  
Working hands-on with real hardware (phones, cameras, sample products), helping to create realistic test setups and environments with various lighting conditions and use cases.
  
  

**Depending on your profile and interests, you may also:**  

Support testing of camera and optics-related use cases, considering glare, reflections, and different lighting setups.
  
Contribute to basic scripting, tools, or small utilities in Python to support automation, data validation, or test setup.
  
  

**You’re a great fit if you have:**  

min 3 years of experience as a QA Engineer or Software Tester.
  
Solid experience testing mobile and web applications end-to-end.
  
Strong understanding of QA fundamentals, best practices, and common tools.
  
Hands-on experience with test automation (e.g. pytest, BrowserStack, or similar frameworks).
  
Practical experience with issue tracking tools and test case management systems.
  
Familiarity with REST APIs and tools like Postman or Swagger.
  
Python programming skills sufficient to write and maintain automated tests and small internal tools (not just “basic” scripts).
  
Familiarity with Git and CI/CD tools (ideally GitHub Actions or similar).
  
Experience testing data-driven systems and validating datasets.
  
Strong exploratory testing skills and the ability to identify edge cases and risky scenarios.
  
Understanding of performance and reliability considerations in distributed/mobile systems.
  
Experience testing across different devices and environments, especially mobile.
  
Good communication skills and ability to work closely with developers, designers, and ML/CV engineers.
  
Strong attention to detail, structured way of working, and an ownership mindset.
  
Ability to work in a fast-paced startup environment with evolving requirements.
  
**Transparent and responsive working style:** you work efficiently and independently while valuing team input, actively seeking feedback, and asking for support when needed.
  
  

**Nice to have:**  

Understanding of computer vision / AI concepts (classification vs detection, datasets, training/validation).
  
Familiarity with evaluating ML model performance (e.g. precision, recall, false positives/negatives).
  
Experience testing AI systems with probabilistic outputs.
  
Familiarity with logs, monitoring tools, and debugging production issues.
  
Ability to think in adversarial scenarios and potential system abuse, including basic security testing.
  
Genuine interest in photography and camera hardware, with an understanding of how different lighting conditions and specular reflections affect image quality.
  
Experience working with camera setups, optics, or other imaging hardware.
  
  

**We are open to different profiles:** you might be a QA engineer with a strong software testing and automation background who is curious about hardware and imaging, or someone with deeper camera/photography experience who wants to grow their software testing skills.
  
  

**What makes us different:**  

**Direct impact:** Your work moves the needle - every idea can become a production feature or a new testing practice.
  
**Trust & teamwork:** A tight, supportive team where collaboration, initiative, and constructive feedback are valued.
  
**Startup energy:** Flexible hours, extra vacation, wellness allowance, many fun team activities, and a cozy office environment in Malmö.
  
**Growth opportunities:** Mentorship, the chance to help shape our QA strategy and processes, and room to expand your role towards automation, tooling, or hardware-focused testing.
  
  

What we’re looking for (mindset)
  
Problem-solver with excellent analytical and open-minded mindset.
  
Self-driven but collaborative - you own your tasks and actively engage with the team.
  
Comfortable switching between structured test plans and exploratory sessions, and between manual and automated testing.
  
Motivated by challenges, eager to learn, and ambitious about your professional growth.
  
  

Additional details
  
Permanent, full-time position.
  
Onsite in Malmö (our expectation is that you primarily work from the office, with some hybrid flexibility possible).
  
Candidates must already be based in Sweden.
  
**Start date:** As soon as possible
  
  

Ready to help shape the future of brand protection and quality assurance for AI-powered, computer-vision solutions?
  
  

Apply via LinkedIn, or send your CV and a short motivation to info@bluecromos.se.

title: Application Engineer
description:
As an Application Engineer at Manta Marine, you will work closely with our vessel optimisation systems, continually adapting and configuring solutions for a wide range of different vessels and onboard components. Your work ensures that each individual installation aligns with the specific technical setup of the vessel.
  
You will be responsible for adjusting configurations using both visual tools and software logic, supporting our commissioning engineers, and verifying functionality through in-office testing. The majority of the configuration work is being transitioned to a visual interface, with only limited coding involved.
  
The role suits someone who enjoys working in a structured and detail-oriented way, and who takes satisfaction in refining solutions that have a direct impact on performance and sustainability.
  
You will join a small and focused team of two other Application Engineers, working closely with colleagues across the company to ensure smooth delivery, testing and commissioning of our optimisation systems.
  
The team maintains a deep understanding of the maritime domain and collaborates regularly to share knowledge and solve challenges. You will work from our Gothenburg office, where we have access to testing equipment and lab setups that mirror real-world vessel installations.
  
Background and skills
  
A technical degree and/or relevant experience, for example within marine engineering, automation or electrical systems
  
Understanding of onboard vessel systems and components
  
Basic knowledge of automation and electrical systems
  
Familiarity with serial communication and protocols like Modbus
  
Comfortable working in English, both spoken and written
  
  

It is beneficial if you have experience with configuration or commissioning of technical systems, experience in software engineering or scripting, or a general understanding of IT infrastructure and networking.
  
Personal qualities
  
We are looking for someone with a strong technical foundation and a genuine interest in marine systems. You may be early in your career or already have relevant experience; what matters most is your motivation to learn and grow within a specialised domain.

title: Research Scientist - Networked Automation Systems
description:
At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

**This position reports to:**


R&D Team Lead
\_\_

**Your Role and Responsibilities**


This role offers a fantastic opportunity to gain a central part in shaping future architectures of ABB’s industrial distributed and networked real-time solutions. The position is based at ABB Corporate Research Center in Västerås, where cutting-edge research meets real industrial impact.


You will belong to a highly motivated, international research team and create next generation industrial distributed and networked real-time systems, while collaborating with researchers, technology and product managers, system architects, and industrial partners. ABB offers an inspiring work environment with excellent opportunities for professional growth and long-term career development.

**Your Responsibilities:**

* Initiate, lead, and participate in research projects focused on innovative industrial distributed and networked real-time systems.
* Design, implement, analyze, and evaluate distributed and networked real-time operating systems.
* Validate ideas and concepts through prototypes and experimental pilots with a clear business relevance.
* Disseminate results through technical reports, patent applications, and scientific publications.
* Collaborate with business stakeholders to translate research results into deployable and impactful solutions.
* Build a professional network of internal and external contacts to actively share and acquire new knowledge across the organization.
**Our Team Dynamics**


Our teams support each other, collaborate, and never stop learning. Everyone brings something unique, and together we push ideas forward to solve real problems. Being part of our team means your work matters - because the progress we make here creates real impact out there.

**Qualifications for the role**

* You hold an MSc or PhD degree in, Software Engineering, Distributed real-time systems, Communication Engineering or a related field.
* Deep knowledge of distributed real-time systems, including theoretical and practical.
* Real-time operating systems, virtualization, and containerization
* Knowledge of Ethernet, IP, and real-time extensions (TSN, DetNet) plus time-synchronization protocols.
* Prototyping expertise in embedded SW/HW systems- Containerized software development.
* Proficiency in software development (C, C++, Python, MATLAB, RTOS)
* Experience with hardware platforms (Raspberry Pi, Arduino, microcontrollers) is a plus.


Mindset

* Eagerness to learn and explore new domains e.q. autonomous systems, AI)
* Curiosity about sustainable energy and automation systems
* Passion for translating research into industrial solutions.
**What’s in it for you?**

We empower you to take initiative, challenge ideas, and lead with confidence. You’ll grow through meaningful work, continuous learning, and support that’s tailored to your goals. Every idea you share and every action you take contributes to something bigger.


ABB provides competitive benefits, ask us!

**More about us**


At ABB Corporate Research Center, we explore new technologies and solutions on behalf of our business areas. We work in a department which is dedicated to creating innovative automation solutions to support ABB and our customers. The research team’s focus is on industrial networks and communication systems, belonging to the Automation Technology Department.


Recruiting Manager Dawid Ziobro, +46 72 245 00 87, will answer your questions about the position. Union representatives – Sveriges Ingenjörer: Carl-Fredrik Lindberg, +46 70 691 10 80; Unionen: Katja Saari, +46 730 77 05 02; Ledarna: Lenny Larsson, +46 706 32 85 47. All other questions can be directed to Talent Partner Irma Leijon, +46 72 461 23 14.


We look forward to reading your application in PDF format. Last day to apply is 23rd of April. Please note that the interview process is ongoing, apply now to secure your spot in the recruitment process!


Please note that to be eligible for employment at ABB Sweden, you will need to pass our pre-employment screening steps. This includes a reference check, a drug test, and could also include an extended background check.


We kindly decline direct contact with staffing and recruitment agencies as well as sellers of additional job advertisements.

**Join us. Be part of the team where progress happens, industries transform, and your work shapes the world.** **Run What Runs the World.**


Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.




Ready to make an impact?


Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Technical Project Manager
description:
We’re looking for a Technical Project Manager who thrives in fast-paced environments and is driven by the challenge of balancing cutting-edge technology with commercial success. Are you great at managing budgets, timelines, planning project resources, and driving the development of new features and products? Then you will thrive in this role.

### **Responsibilities**

* **Lead with both technical expertise and commercial insight:** Balance technical complexity with the need to create value and drive projects to market.
* **Take ownership**: Manage your projects from start to finish, ensuring timelines, budgets, and customer expectations are met while navigating the ever-changing landscape of our industry.
* **Collaborate and communicate**: Be the key point of contact between internal teams and customers, ensuring alignment on goals and expectations. Your ability to communicate clearly will be essential.
* **Drive innovation and efficiency**: Lead teams to work lean and focus on the minimum viable product while continuously finding opportunities to add value for our customers.

### **Requirements**

* 2-4 years of relevant experience, preferably in a role such as Project Manager, Lead Engineer, QA Engineer, or Technical Team Lead.
* Master’s degree (MSc) or Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or Industrial Engineering.
* A solid technical foundation, with a background in software and/or mechanical engineering,
* Strong knowledge of the software domain, primarily embedded systems, and an understanding of integrated systems and end-to-end product development.
* Fluency in both Swedish and English is preferred.


The position requires an on-site presence four days per week, with the option to work remotely one day per week.

### **Who are you?**


We believe you are someone who is naturally structured and organized, with a strong sense of order and attention to detail. You enjoy working with documentation and understand the importance of clear, well-structured technical information in ensuring alignment and quality. You communicate openly and transparently, building trust with both internal and external stakeholders. You take genuine ownership of your responsibilities, show initiative, and are comfortable making decisions.


With a solution-oriented mindset, you enjoy coordinating across multiple stakeholders, keeping everyone aligned and working towards common goals. We believe you bring a few years of experience from similar responsibilities, giving you the confidence to navigate challenges in this role.


You will be working with products connected to our marine segment, so an interest in or previous experience related to boats is naturally a strong plus.

### **About us**


CPAC Systems develops advanced control systems for marine and industrial applications. Since 1999, we’ve been turning complex operations into intuitive experiences. With 250 employees, a portfolio of +80 patents and more than 500,000 delivered products per year, we drive innovation in everything from electrification to intelligent automation. As part of Volvo Group, our role is to lead advanced technology development and explore the next generation of smart control systems.

### **Why CPAC Systems?**

* Work on exciting projects and drive them end to end.
* Thrive in a flat organization with short decision-making paths.
* Join a team with deep technical expertise and a culture that values initiative and collaboration
* Enjoy a fun and supportive team oriented environment.

### **Others**


If you're excited to make a significant impact and want to be a part of our innovative team, we'd love to learn more about you. Don't hesitate to reach out to our recruiting manager william.lindh@cpacsystems.se with any questions you may have.

title: Expert Deep Learning Engineer
description:
*Do you want to design and train deep neural networks from scratch? Are you excited about building frameworks for end-to-end learning that make self-driving cars safer? Are you looking for a place to collaborate with experts in your field, in a highly innovative company where you can enjoy genuine work life balance? Do you want to work with the latest technologies like transformers, in* *a warm inclusive culture - and help save millions of lives in the process? Apply today - no CV needed, all we need is your name, email and phone number! We'll cover the rest later.*

### **Insights from the team**


At Zenseact, we design and train spatio-temporal neural networks for end-to-end driving — **systems that already run in our cars today.** Our models are deployed on a single compute platform, processing multi-modal sensor data in real time and making safety-critical decisions on the road. We are a team of engineers and researchers working on the next generation of deep learning for autonomous driving. We work across the full stack: from tailored data pipelines and large-scale training to architecture design, validation and production integration. We train networks from the ground up, using tailored datasets and custom architectures. Collaboration and curiosity drive us forward.


Solving these complex problems is a team effort — researchers and engineers learning from each other, sharing knowledge openly, and growing through hands-on challenges in production systems. Our mission is to create models that understand complex environments and make safe decisions in real time.


The next major release — including the upcoming EX60 in summer 2026 — will further evolve our AI stack in real-world cars. Here, you combine cutting-edge AI with a culture that values collaboration, flexibility and balance — making space for life outside of work while building technology that truly makes a difference on the road.

### **Your mission and day-to-day tasks**


In this role, you will focus on end-to-end learning approaches for autonomous driving. You’ll design spatio-temporal neural networks, train them on large-scale data, and integrate them into production systems. You’ll collaborate closely with your peers – experts in perception, planning, and software engineering, to share ideas and deliver solutions that matter. Together you’re solving the hardest problems in autonomous driving, training AI to predict the unpredictable.

* Develop and optimize deep learning models for end-to-end driving
* Build scalable pipelines for data processing and model training
* Experiment with architectures such as transformers and recurrent networks
* Collaborate across teams to deploy models into real-world scenarios

#### **What’s in it for you**

* Work at the forefront of deep tech in one of the biggest and most interesting, challenging and dynamic AI/ML-companies in Northern Europe.
* Collaborate and team-work with highly skilled, innovative, creative and smart people.
* Work with people from all over the world, close to what science just came up with – or innovate it yourself.
* Enjoy a flexible work approach and a **genuinely** good work/life balance.

### **Qualifications & experience**


We’re looking for someone who combines technical depth with a sense of responsibility for the bigger picture. You should be analytical, a problem solver, and an active listener who values teamwork and humility.

##### **Essential experience**

* Deep knowledge of spatio-temporal neural networks
* Strong background in deep learning, software development, and data engineering
* Proficiency in Python and frameworks like TensorFlow or PyTorch
* Solid understanding of statistics and geometry

##### **Meriting experience**

* Transformers, RNNs, multi-head attention
* Perception systems (computer vision, lidar, radar)
* Tracking and sensor fusion
* Experience with imitation learning or behavior cloning

 *This role may have access to sensitive information, trade secrets, and confidential data. As part of the recruitment process, the selected candidate might undergo a background check.*

#### **More about Zenseact**

 **Our software makes a difference.**  

Using AI-based technology to create the ultimate driver support, we’re fighting to end car accidents and make roads safe for everyone. Every year, around 1,4 million people die in traffic while approximately 50 million people get injured. Many get disabled as a result of their injury. We can do better.

**One purpose, one product.**  

We’re a software company dedicated to revolutionizing car safety. By designing the complete software stack for autonomous driving and advanced driver-assistance systems, we’re fighting to end car accidents and make roads safe for everyone. Zenseact was founded by Volvo Cars, and the teams are based in Gothenburg and Lund, Sweden and Munich in Germany. When we aim for zero accidents faster, we strive to speed up the transition to safe automation. This is essentially achieved by making cars updatable – like a computer or a phone. With regular software updates, a vehicle can be made safer long after its production. By accelerating improvement loops, shortening development cycles, and deploying high-capacity software quickly, we can make cars safer, and faster.

**Culture with people at heart**  

To achieve our mission of saving lives and ending traffic accidents we must go where nobody has before. It requires us to venture into the unknown, pioneering new technology and pushing the frontier of autonomous driving. While there’s no denying our determination and expertise, we must stand united to succeed. By fostering a culture of support and enablement – a place of psychological safety where all of us can thrive – everything else will follow. We call this a **people-at-heart culture.** This culture means caring. It means the company cares about me, and we care about one another. It means sharing, so we give each other energy and have fun together. Our culture is also about belonging. It’s important to feel at home and that we can be ourselves at work. Finally, a people-at-heart culture means well-being. So, we enjoy the flexibility needed to be and do our best – at work and in life.

 *Zenseact works proactively to create a culture of diversity and inclusion, where individual differences are appreciated and respected. To drive innovation we see diversity as an asset, which means we value and respect differences in gender, race, ethnicity, religion or other belief, disability, sexual orientation or age, etc.*  

  

*Interviews are held continuously, so we highly recommend that you submit your application at your earliest convenience.*

title: Quality Assurance Engineer
description:
Vill du vara med och forma framtidens bankupplevelse? Hos oss på NOBA får du chansen att bidra till en tryggare och smidigare vardag för våra kunder – samtidigt som du utvecklas tillsammans med engagerade kollegor i ett techdrivet team.
  

  

NOBA Bank Group är en ledande europeisk specialistbank med tre starka varumärken – Nordax Bank, Bank Norwegian och Svensk Hypotekspension. Med över två miljoner kunder kombinerar vi storlek, expertis och skalbarhet för att bidra till bättre finansiell hälsa för fler. NOBA samlar specialiserade, kundcentrerade finansiella erbjudanden som är hållbara för individen, banken och samhället – både idag och i framtiden.


Hos oss får du vara med och påverka, både din egen utveckling och våra produkter. Vi erbjuder en modern arbetsplats med flexibla arbetstider (2 dagar/vecka på kontoret), kompetenta kollegor och möjlighet att växa tillsammans med oss.  

  

**Om rollen:**  

I rollen som Quality Assurance Engineer blir du en del av vårt QA-team på sex personer inom avdelningen Software Engineering. Teamet består av erfarna kollegor med flera års erfarenhet inom test och kvalitetssäkring, och vi värdesätter en kultur präglad av öppenhet, samarbete och en positiv arbetsmiljö, samtidigt som vi alltid strävar efter hög kvalitet i det vi levererar.

  

Som QA Engineer hos oss arbetar du nära utvecklare, produktägare och andra QA-kollegor för att säkerställa att våra digitala lösningar håller hög kvalitet. Teamet ansvarar för testning av både bankens interna system och system från externa leverantörer, vilket ger en varierad roll där du får arbeta brett över olika delar av vår tekniska miljö.


Vi ser kvalitet som ett lagarbete och tror att nyfikenhet, noggrannhet och samarbete är avgörande för att lyckas!  

  

**Huvudsakliga uppgifter:**

* Utföra manuella tester samt skriva och underhålla testfall.
* Utforska och testa nya funktioner för att säkerställa en stabil och användarvänlig produkt.
* Skapa tydliga och reproducerbara buggrapporter samt verifiera och följa upp åtgärder.
* Delta i kravdiskussioner och testplanering tillsammans med utvecklingsteamet.
* Bidra till att utveckla och förbättra våra QA-processer
* Stötta vid användartester och koordinera testaktiviteter med relevanta intressenter.

**Vi söker dig som:**  

I den här rollen värdesätter vi domänkunskap inom bank och finansiella produkter lika högt som erfarenhet av test. Vi söker därför en person som har god förståelse för banksystem och användarflöden, gärna kopplat till core banking-plattformar och låneprodukter.


Det är inte avgörande att du har arbetat specifikt i en QA-roll tidigare, men du behöver ha en förståelse för mjukvarutestning, utvecklingsprocesser och hur kvalitetssäkring fungerar i praktiken. Du har också en god affärsförståelse och kan sätta dig in i hur bankprodukter fungerar ur både kund- och verksamhetsperspektiv.

**Vi ser gärna att du har:**

* Erfarenhet av manuella tester (t.ex. funktionella tester, regression, smoke eller exploratory testing)
* Erfarenhet av core banking-plattformar, gärna kopplat till låneprodukter
* Förståelse för mjukvaruutvecklingsprocesser
* Flytande i både svenska och engelska (tal och skrift)

**Vi ser det som positivt om du:**

* Erfarenhet av verktyg som Jira och API-testning (t.ex. Postman)
* Erfarenhet av att läsa och redigera XML-filer
* Vana att läsa loggar och använda browser developer tool
* Erfarenhet av testdata- och miljöhantering
* Erfarenhet av att ta fram testdokumentation eller revisionsspårbar testdata
* Grundläggande skriptning (t.ex. Python eller Bash)

**För att trivas och prestera väl i rollen tror vi att du behöver vara:**  

Nyfiken på kvalitet och värdet av mjukvarutestning. Du trivs i en teammiljö där samarbete och kunskapsdelning är en naturlig del av arbetet, och du har en god kommunikativ förmåga. Med ett strukturerat arbetssätt och ett noggrant öga för detaljer bidrar du till att säkerställa hög kvalitet i de lösningar vi levererar.  

  

Utöver detta är det viktigt för oss att du känner igen dig i våra värderingar: vi är helhjärtade, vi samarbetar och vi tar ledningen.

 **Har du hört om NOBAVERSE?** I vårt NOBAVERSE värdesätter vi öppenhet, tillit och mångfald. Hos oss får du påverka din utveckling och ha roligt på vägen mot våra mål – allt i en lite extra bubbly miljö! Läs mer om vårt medarbetarlöfte på vår karriärsida.

**Läs mer om vår rekryteringsprocess i vår** **FAQ****.**


Vi vill, med hänvisning till GDPR, be dig att inte inkludera några känsliga personuppgifter i din ansökan, exempelvis information om etniskt ursprung, politiska åsikter, religiös övertygelse eller hälsa.

**Läs mer om hur vi hanterar dina personuppgifter** **här****.**

  

Om du har några frågor kring tjänsten kan du kontakta ansvarig rekryterare.  

  

Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!

title: Software Engineer - Java/Kotlin
description:
We are looking for a Software Engineer with a passion for great and innovative solutions and a desire to help build a truly digital organization. In this role, you will design, develop, and maintain a robust microservice infrastructure, while also leveraging AI-driven and low-code platforms to rapidly deliver targeted, high-impact solutions. You will focus on creating tailored integrations and workflows that support our business goals with both speed and scalability.
   

  

 As this role supports a dynamic construction project, you will need to thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment. Flexibility, quick problem-solving, and the ability to manage changing priorities are key to your success. You will work closely with domain experts to understand their workflows and develop solutions that address their specific needs.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Design, develop, and maintain scalable microservices architecture.
* Leverage AI and low-code platforms to rapidly deliver focused, business-driven solutions and workflows.
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver tailored solutions and integrations.
* Maintain and improve DevSecOps procedures, ensuring strong governance and security policies.
* Work with domain experts to translate business requirements into technical solutions.
* Adapt and respond quickly to shifting project priorities and timelines in a high-paced environment.
* Actively contribute to the growth and knowledge-sharing within your team.


**What We’re Looking For**
* Ability to work efficiently in a high-paced, evolving project environment.
* A collaborative mindset with a desire to work closely with colleagues across different functions.
* Experience working with low-code platforms and/or AI-assisted development tools to deliver fast, high-quality solutions.
* Experience with Java/Kotlin and Spring Boot frameworks or similar (3-5 years preferably from being a consultant or within start-up companies).
* A strong understanding of modern Backend architecture principles.
* An eagerness to learn and contribute beyond your core technical expertise.


 Start date: 2026-04-20
   

  

 End date: 2026-12-31
   

  

 Workplace: Stockholm - 2 days onsite.

title: Software Engineers to Alva Labs
description:
**TL;DR**
 Alva Labs is looking for Software Engineers to help shape the future of hiring. In this role, you’ll work across their stack to build products that make hiring fair, predictive, and delightful — for everyone.
   

  

**Why Alva?**
 At Alva Labs, they’re redefining how companies hire — replacing gut feeling with science. They’re building an AI-first Hiring System: the only platform companies need to define roles, assess candidates, make confident decisions, and continuously improve their hiring.
   

  

 Instead of fragmented tools, they’re creating one cohesive system designed to make hiring accurate, fair, and scalable. Leading companies across Europe trust Alva Labs to make hiring more accurate, inclusive, and scalable.
   

  

 The team is a small, ambitious group based in Stockholm, Sweden. They value ownership, velocity, and low-ego collaboration. They care deeply about their craft and about each other — and they’re driven to build something that lasts.
   

  

 Joining now means being part of the growth journey as Alva Labs scales their product, explores new AI-powered capabilities, and brings fair hiring to millions more people.
   

  

**What They’re Looking For**
* Solid experience building and shipping products end-to-end
* Strong skills in modern frontend (React/TypeScript) and backend development (Python/Node.js)
* Solid understanding of software design principles and writing scalable, performant code
* Product mindset — you balance speed with long-term thinking and make well-reasoned decisions
* Collaborative spirit — you thrive in cross-functional teams with product, design, data, and ML


**What You’ll Do**
**In one sentence:** 
 Build the core Alva experience and take it to the next level.
   

  

* Design, build, and maintain features across the stack — frontend, backend, and infrastructure
* Take ownership of features end-to-end: from technical design to implementation, deployment, and iteration
* Collaborate closely with data scientists, designers, ML engineers, and product managers to turn ideas into intuitive product experiences
* Drive projects through the full lifecycle: planning, shipping, learning, and improving


**Tech Stack**
 They use tools that keep them fast, stable, and happy:
   

  

**Frontend:** 
 React, TypeScript
   

  

**Backend:** 
 Python, Node.js
   

  

**Infrastructure:** 
 Google Cloud Platform, Kubernetes, and various LLM providers
   

  

**Data & Analytics:** 
 Python, BigQuery, dbt
   

  

**DevOps & Tooling:** 
 GitHub, Terraform
   

  

**Questions and contact**
 Alva Labs is working with Amendo in this recruitment process. If you have any questions regarding this specific role, please contact Business Area Manager Johanna Hallenberg at johanna.hallenberg@amendo.se. Interviews are conducted on a rolling basis, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible. Please submit your application in English, as it is the company language at Alva Labs
   

  

**About Amendo**
 Amendo is one of Sweden’s leading recruitment and consulting company, with a clear focus on accounting, banking, finance, insurance, IT, and tech roles and expertise. We help companies grow and people develop - we are your trusted and dedicated talent partner.
   

  

 Follow us on
 **Linkedin** 
 ,
 **Instagram** 
 and
 **Facebook** 
 .
   

  

 #IT
   

  

 Amendo har arbetat med framtidens kompetens sedan 2001 och är landets ledande konsult- och rekryteringsföretag inom ekonomi, bank, finans, försäkring, IT och tech. Vi är ett expansivt bolag verksamma över hela landet med lokala kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg och Örnsköldsvik.
   

  

 Vår vision att utveckla världen med kompetens når vi genom att arbeta nära våra kunder och kandidater, vara nyfikna på omvärlden - och hela tiden vilja framåt.
   

  

 Läs mer på amendo.se.

title: Engineering Lead
description:
**Engineering Lead – Stockholm (Digital‑First · Product Team in Insurance)**




**Location:** 
 Stockholm
 



**Employment:** 
 Permanent
 



**Team Size:** 
 ~100 people company‑wide, ~20 in tech & product
 



**Domain:** 
 Digital insurance · Mobile-first · AI‑enhanced products
 



  





 A rapidly growing digital‑first insurance scaleup is looking for an Engineering Lead to help shape the next generation of their mobile and web platform. The company has over strong customer base and is known for disrupting a traditional industry through cutting‑edge tech, automation, and a highly customer‑centric product experience. They are financially stable, organically growing, and now expanding both their product capabilities and the use of AI across their ecosystem.
 



  





**What They Offer** 




 A suite of personal insurance products (home, car, pet, student, accident), all delivered through a seamless digital experience. Their mobile‑first platform enables:
 



 • Fully digital claims (voice + photo uploads)
 



 • Fast support directly through the app
 



 • Transparent pricing and no long contracts
 



 • High user satisfaction and strong reputation among younger digital-native customers
 



  





**About the Role – Engineering Lead** 




 As an Engineering Lead, you will guide a team of engineers, combining technical leadership with people development and delivery excellence. You will foster a high‑performing team environment, ensure sustainable ways of working, and support strategic technical decisions that align with the company’s long‑term vision.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities**




**People & Team Development:** 




 • Coach and develop engineers with continuous feedback and structured development plans
 



 • Build a psychologically safe, collaborative and high-performing team culture
 



 • Support growth opportunities and drive skill development within the team
 



**Team Execution & Delivery:** 




 • Ensure efficient, sustainable delivery processes together with the PM
 



 • Promote knowledge sharing to prevent silos
 



 • Support resource planning, staffing and capacity management
 



 • Help set realistic estimates, timelines and delivery expectations
 



**Technical Leadership:** 




 • Guide engineers in sound technical decision-making
 



 • Lead architecture and design discussions
 



 • Identify and communicate technical risks
 



 • Ensure incidents are followed up with proper post-mortems
 



 • Drive reduction of technical debt and investment in stable infrastructure
 



 • Contribute hands-on where needed to support end-to-end feature delivery
 



  





**Key Requirements:**




 • Not a pure people manager (you combine personnel leadership with being a hands‑on individual contributor and technical lead.)
 



 • A leader who inspires trust, clarity and momentum, while still enjoying coding and staying close to modern engineering practices.
 



 • Someone who cares deeply about healthy team culture, strong engineering standards, smart delivery processes and scaling good practices.
 



 • Curious about emerging technologies, including how AI can improve engineering workflows, team productivity and product impact.
 



 • Experience using or experimenting with AI tools in projects or organisations is a plus
 



**To Succeed in This Role, You:** 




 • Have 4–5+ years of professional experience as a software engineer with proven coaching/mentoring responsibilities.
 



 • Have recent hands‑on coding experience and feel comfortable contributing to production code when needed.
 



 • Can balance strategic leadership with practical, tactical involvement in the team’s day‑to‑day work.
 



 • Communicate clearly, give constructive feedback and collaborate well across disciplines.
 



 • Are familiar with agile methodologies and the full software development lifecycle.
 



 • Have a strong technical foundation and solid computer science fundamentals.
 



 • Align with the organisation’s values and technical vision, and want to help shape how the engineering function evolves.
 



  





**Why This Role Is Attractive** 




 • Join a modern, mission-driven tech company reshaping a large, traditional industry
 



 • Work in a mobile-first, product-led, consumer-facing environment
 



 • Strong organisational stability with continuous organic growth
 



 • Real influence on engineering culture, technical direction and team development
 



 • Opportunity to help scale AI-driven capabilities that bring genuine user value

title: Test Leader and Tester
description:
Vi har nu ett nytt behov och söker därför en konsult och kollega med nedan kvalifikationer. Tjänsten är en del av vår konsultverksamhet vilket innebär att du blir anställd av oss och arbetar hos kund alt. interna projekt och åtaganden.
   

  

**Din profil (Skall krav)**
* Erfaren SW testledare, gärna från fordonsindustrin eller annan bransch med regulatoriska krav inom säkerhet och cybersäkerhet
* Erfarenhet av embedded‑system och testfall framtagna för inbyggd mjukvara
* Kunskap och erfarenhet av:
* Embedded C
* Robot Framework
* Python
* Jenkins
* Jira
* Kravhanteringsverktyg såsom Visure eller Polarion


**Sista ansökningdatum:** 
 08-04-2026
   

  

**Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**
 🚀 Spännande uppdrag hos våra många kunder. Vi fokuserar på långsiktiga uppdrag, vilket ger dig möjlighet att skapa värde för kunden samtidigt som du utvecklas själv.
   

  

 🌟 En trygg månadslön – tillsammans bestämmer vi en grundlön baserad på dina uppdrag.
   

  

 💼 Tjänstepension och stora möjligheter till löneväxling.

 🏥 Privat sjukvårdsförsäkring, olycksfallsförsäkring och livförsäkring för ökad trygghet.
   

  

 📚 Kompetensutveckling – du får själv sätta din budget och välja vilka utbildningar du vill delta i.
   

  

 🕑 Livskvalitet - Har du kanske svårt att få ihop pusslet i vardagen och önskar arbeta lite mindre och ändå kunna tjäna lika mycket om inte mer?
   

  

* Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande, med hänsyn till höga arbetstrycket är det extra viktigt med en kort motivering för tjänsten som förklarar hur man möter kraven.

title: Software Engineer - Ubuntu Server Certification
description:
Canonical is hiring a software engineer to join the Server Certification team. The Certification team develops Python based testing tools used to test Ubuntu Server on the latest enterprise hardware ensuring Ubuntu users have the best experience with their hardware. We work at the interface of hardware and software, so the ideal candidate is a Python developer with a problem solving mindset, familiar with hardware.
   

  

 Server Certification team represents Canonical to our datacenter hardware partners. We work together with silicon companies, OEMs, ODMs and teams within Canonical to ensure a smooth, secure out of the box Ubuntu experience, with success in this role leading to a great Ubuntu experience being offered on more and more devices. We focus on the interaction between server hardware and Ubuntu Linux, so the ideal candidate has software development experience and a good understanding of configuring and troubleshooting server hardware running Linux.
   

  

 This is an excellent opportunity for someone who wants to have a meaningful impact on the adoption of Linux on enterprise server hardware across multiple industries. Canonical offers a fun, fast-paced team environment and a career full of learning and development opportunities. This team is centered in North and South American time zones.
   

  

 What you'll do
   

  

* Contribute to Canonical's open source testing tools such as Checkbox and Testflinger.
* Troubleshoot issues related to hardware compatibility, functionality, and performance.
* Technically support Canonical's hardware partners in their hardware certification efforts.
* Educate the partner about Canonical's tools, processes, and product roadmaps.
* Support Canonical's internal teams on customer deployments of Canonical's suite of Datacenter and Cloud products that utilize the partner's hardware.
* Work from home with global travel 2 to 4 times per year for internal and external events.


 Who you are
   

  

* You have a good knowledge of Python and shell scripting.
* You have knowledge of server-class hardware.
* You know your way around Ubuntu, virtualization, and cloud computing concepts.
* You are confident in a customer-facing role.


**Desired Skills And Experience**
* Experience with test automation tools for validating hardware compatibility.
* Knowledge of CI/CD concepts.
* Comfortable with some virtualization or container technologies such as QEMU, KVM, LXC / LXD, Docker.


*We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. We truly believe that diversity of experience, perspectives, and background will lead to a better environment for our employees and a better platform for our users and customers. This is something we value deeply and we encourage everyone to come be a part of the world of Ubuntu.*

title: Staff Software Engineer, Core Engine
description:
**The opportunity**
 We're looking for a Staff Engineer to join the Runtime team within the Core Engine group here at Unity. You'll be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining the low-level systems that form the backbone of the Unity runtime. You'll work across subsystems including transforms, the player loop, the job system, serialization, and support higher level features like physics and netcode. Your work will directly impact the performance, stability, and capabilities available to millions of developers worldwide.
   

  

 This is a highly technical individual contributor role with significant influence over architectural decisions and engineering practices. You'll collaborate closely with teams across rendering, physics, animation, scripting, and platform engineering to ensure the core engine meets the evolving needs of real-time 3D content creation.
   

  

 This role requires deep expertise in C++ and systems programming, a track record of technical leadership, and the ability to mentor engineers while driving critical initiatives that shape how creators build with Unity.
   

  

**What You'll Be Doing**
* Design and implement core runtime systems with a focus on performance, memory efficiency, and cross-platform compatibility
* Lead technical initiatives spanning multiple teams, driving alignment on architecture and implementation approaches
* Profile, analyze, and optimize engine performance across diverse hardware targets including consoles, mobile devices, desktop, and emerging platforms
* Mentor and support engineers across the organization, raising the bar for code quality and engineering rigor
* Contribute to technical strategy and roadmap planning for the core engine
* Write clear technical documentation and participate in design reviews
* Debug complex, system-level issues that span multiple subsystems
* Evaluate and integrate new platform capabilities and hardware features


**What We're Looking For**
* Extensive experience in systems programming, with deep expertise in C++
* Proven experience building and shipping low-level engine or runtime systems at scale
* Strong understanding of computer architecture, memory hierarchies, cache behavior, and CPU/GPU interaction
* Experience with multithreaded programming
* Familiarity with multiple platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux, consoles, mobile) and their toolchains
* Track record of leading cross-team technical initiatives and influencing engineering direction
* Excellent debugging and profiling skills
* Strong written and verbal communication skills


**You might also have**
* Experience with game engine development (Unity, Unreal, proprietary engines)
* Experience with C# and the managed runtime
* Familiarity with SIMD programming
* Experience with Burst, LLVM, or other compiler technologies
* Contributions to open-source projects or published technical writing


**Additional Information**
* Relocation support is not available for this position


**Benefits**
 At Unity, we want our team members to thrive. We offer a wide range of benefits designed to support well-being and work-life balance.
   

  

 Please note: Benefits eligibility, specific offerings, and coverage vary based on the country and employment status.
   

  

 While specific benefits vary, here are some of the ways we strive to take care of our eligible team members globally: Comprehensive health, life, and disability insurance | Commute subsidy | Employee stock ownership | Competitive retirement/pension plans | Generous vacation and personal days | Support for new parents through leave and family-care programs | Office food snacks | Mental Health and Wellbeing programs and support | Employee Resource Groups | Global Employee Assistance Program | Training and development programs | Volunteering and donation matching program
   

  

**Life at Unity**
 Unity [NYSE: U] is the world’s leading game engine, powering play for more than 3 billion consumers each month. The top mobile games in the world, the most played PC indie titles, the most innovative console games, and virtually all of the top XR and Web Games are developed, deployed, and grown in Unity. Unity also enables teams across industries like automotive, manufacturing, and healthcare to design, simulate, and collaborate in 3D — closing the gap between ideas and reality. For more information, please visit www.unity.com.
   

  

*Unity is a proud equal opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive, innovative environment and celebrate our employees across age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law. title: Senior C++ Engineer - Platform Engineering
description:
The Synchrotron squad, part of the Client Platform Studio, plays a key role in providing the architectural foundation for our mobile apps. We’re looking for a passionate and skilled IOS/C++ Engineer to join the band and help us scale our platform for the future.
   

  

 As our app codebase continues to grow, we’re committed to keeping development smooth, efficient, and joyful for our feature engineers. That’s where you come in! Your role will be instrumental in shaping our architecture to ensure developers can work within isolated systems, reducing cognitive load and build complexity—so they can focus on building the features our users love. You'll be working on establishing and maintaining building blocks that help teams across Spotify reason about application state and logic flow with clarity.
   

  

 You’ll collaborate closely with teams across the Client Platform Studio, including feature developers, build configuration owners, and infrastructure engineers. Ensuring that our platform continues to empower innovation at scale. If you’re passionate about creating tools and systems that let developers thrive, come help us make Spotify even better for the millions of people who rely on it every day.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Join a collaborative and autonomous team of engineers dedicated to improving the mobile app architecture at Spotify
* Implement and maintain isolation strategies for SEM (Software Ecosystem Model) conformance within repositories and across multiple repositories, ensuring that systems are designed to be developed and tested in isolation to improve build performance and reduce interdependencies.
* Enable flawless interoperability between C++ within the IOS codebase, demonstrating modern language capabilities to enhance software development efficiency and maintainability.
* Use established service systems (from platform or in-house solutions) to manage application services and their lifecycles effectively, ensuring that services are robust, scalable, and maintainable.
* Guide the composition of the app by thoughtfully integrating and using features and building blocks sourced from various parts of the organization, with a focus on improving product quality through foundational building blocks.
* Promote sound engineering practices, drive work on technical strategy and roadmaps.


 Who You Are
   

  

* Proficiency C++ is required, and ideally also some knowledge in iOS development. Having expertise in both is a significant advantage. Candidates should demonstrate strong proficiency in C++, along with some experience in iOS, and a strong willingness to further deepen their knowledge.
* A great teammate who takes initiative and has the ability to see the bigger picture, especially when it comes to improving developer experience
* Strong understanding of software architecture and design principles
* Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail
* Strong communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with multi-functional teams
* Experience with modular codebases and code isolation strategies
* Knowledge of service-oriented architecture and lifecycle management


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Testledare
description:
Vi har nu ett nytt behov och söker därför en konsult och kollega med nedan kvalifikationer. Tjänsten är en del av vår konsultverksamhet vilket innebär att du blir anställd av oss och arbetar hos kund alt. interna projekt och åtaganden.
   

  

**Din profil (Skall krav)**
* Högskole-/universitetsutbildning 120p/180hp inom IT, teknik, data och/eller systemvetenskap, alternativt motsvarande arbetslivserfarenhet från IT-området.
* ISTQB-certifiering
* Minst 10 års erfarenhet från de senaste 15 åren i roller som testledare inom IT. Arbete ska ha omfattat testning av både funktionella och icke-funktionella krav och planering, styrning och uppföljning av testarbete genom hela IT-livscykeln
* Minst 5 års arbetslivserfarenhet från de senaste 10 åren av testning eller testledning av infrastruktur och applikationer i en komplex och säkerhetskänslig IT-miljö
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet från de senaste 10 åren av riskbaserad testning och bedömning av kvalitets- och leveransrisker
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet från de senaste 10 åren av att ta fram, underhålla och exekvera testplaner för High Availability (HA) och Disaster Recovery (DR) för end-to-end-lösningar över en hel teknikstack (d.v.s applikationer och dess underliggande infrastruktur).
* God skriftlig och muntlig kommunikationsförmåga på svenska och engelska


**Meriterande**
* Minst 3 års arbetslivserfarenhet från arbete med test i reglerade miljöer (polis, försvar, bank/finans, energi, sjukvård).
* Minst 3 års arbetslivserfarenhet av att arbeta i ett tvärfunktionellt team med agila metoder och principer.


**Sista ansökningdatum:** 
 07-04-2026
   

  

**Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**
 🚀 Spännande uppdrag hos våra många kunder. Vi fokuserar på långsiktiga uppdrag, vilket ger dig möjlighet att skapa värde för kunden samtidigt som du utvecklas själv.
   

  

 🌟 En trygg månadslön – tillsammans bestämmer vi en grundlön baserad på dina uppdrag.
   

  

 💼 Tjänstepension och stora möjligheter till löneväxling.
   

  

 🌴 Semester - Önskar du mer än 30 dagar semester, kanske 60 dagar?
   

  

 🏥 Privat sjukvårdsförsäkring, olycksfallsförsäkring och livförsäkring för ökad trygghet.
   

  

 📚 Kompetensutveckling – du får själv sätta din budget och välja vilka utbildningar du vill delta i.
   

  

 🕑 Livskvalitet - Har du kanske svårt att få ihop pusslet i vardagen och önskar arbeta lite mindre och ändå kunna tjäna lika mycket om inte mer?
   

  

* Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande, med hänsyn till höga arbetstrycket är det extra viktigt med en kort motivering för tjänsten som förklarar hur man möter kraven.

title: Forward Deployed Engineer
description:
**Build something monumental for Healthcare!**
 At Tandem Health we’re reimagining healthcare by putting clinicians first. Our platform - designed by clinicians, for clinicians - is built on deep insight into real-world pain points, with intuitive medical notes and workflows that truly support patient care.
   

  

 We’re a fast-scaling health-tech company backed by top investors and expanding globally. We move fast, stay curious, and believe building something that matters starts with an extraordinary team. If you're passionate about impact and innovation, we'd love to meet you!
   

  

**About The Role**
 You love making things work. You're not in love with a specific solution or technology, but rather delivering value to users.
   

  

 At Tandem, Forward Deployed Engineers are the ones who bridge the gap between
 *the product demo* 
 and
 *real clinicians delivering better more efficient care* 
 . You’ll take on whatever stands in the way of our product’s real-world impact, debugging integrations, tweaking deployments, writing code to fill the gaps, and making sure everything runs smoothly in our customers’ environments. The learnings and abstractions you build along the way will set the foundation for the next generation of the product offering.
   

  

 Making things work for real is often messy & painful - but that’s what makes it fun! The prestige comes from happy customers not using the latest shiny dev tool.
   

  

 You’ll see firsthand how the tech you build actually changes how healthcare works, and you’ll become one of the people that truly understand both the technical and operational sides of what we do.
   

  

 This role is perfect for someone who’s obsessed with solving problems, who codes because they love it, and who cares more about
 *impact* 
 than tech stack. It’s a role for builders - people with founder energy, who want to learn fast, ship fast, and see their work make a difference immediately.
   

  

 If that sounds like you, we’d love to meet you.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Work with deploying, integrating, and optimizing our product in customer environments.
* Debug and solve complex, real-world problems across infrastructure, APIs, data flows, RPA, and configuration.
* Write clean, pragmatic code to adapt and extend our systems for each unique use case.
* Collaborate directly with clinicians, partners, and our internal teams to remove blockers and make deployments seamless.
* Shape the next generation of our product such that the next rollout delivers more value, more smoothly.
* Continuously identify, automate, and improve the painful parts of deployment so we can scale smarter next time.


**What You Bring**
* Deep love for programming and problem-solving.
* Curiosity that borders on obsession - you like understanding why things work (or don’t).
* A bias toward action - you’d rather get things done than talk about them.
* Comfort working in ambiguity and high velocity.
* A mindset that leans toward ownership, creativity, and future-founder energy.


 Bonus Points
   

  

* Experience integrating or deploying complex products in healthcare.
* Any other language skills than a fluency in English is a plus, especially German.
* Solid experience coding in Python, TypeScript, or C#.
* Experience with Robotic Process Automation (RPA) in the web or Windows context.


**Location**
 We believe the best ideas happen when we’re together. This role is based in our vibrant HQ in the Epicenter building in the heart of Stockholm where you will work primarily from our office to collaborate, connect, and build our culture.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 We adopt a continuous selection process, so please make sure to apply with your CV in English.
   

  

 Our interview process consists of 4 stages:
   

  

* Screening interview with Talent Acquisition
* Technical interviews with some of our Engineers
* Interview with our CTO & Co-Founder
* Home case and presentation at our office - Join us in the Stockholm office for a lunch, a presentation and som pair programming to experience our our culture firsthand, collaborate with our team, and see how you work in action.


**Benefits**
* Competitive salary & company stock options
* 25 days/year of paid vacation
* Pension
* 5,000 SEK wellness allowance (friskvårdsbidrag)
* Social and team-building activities (off-sites, after works, winter/summer parties)
* An opportunity to make a real positive impact in the world of healthcare
* Work with some of the best minds in AI, healthcare, and engineering.


*We review our benefits packages on a regular basis and might modify our benefits from time to time.*
**Culture at Tandem**
 At Tandem, we move fast, think big, and take ownership. We're a high-performing, diverse team with a shared drive to change the future of healthcare - and we’re just getting started.
   

  

 Our culture is built on action, ambition, and learning. You'll be trusted to take the lead, challenge yourself, and make an impact from day one. We believe real growth happens when you're stretched, supported, and surrounded by smart, passionate teammates who want to win together.
   

  

 Even though we’re spread across countries, we come together often in Sweden for team meetings, social events, and offsites - blending global reach with real human connection.
   

  

 We hire for talent, potential, and attitude - valuing different backgrounds and fresh perspectives. Great ideas come from everywhere, and we’re building a team that reflects the world we want to change.
   

  

*Tandem handles sensitive patient data and will conduct a background check before hiring any candidate.*

title: Dynamics 365 CE Specialist
description:
**Dynamics 365 CE Specialist – Global Transformation Programme (Stockholm)** 




  





 A leading international industrial group is embarking on one of the largest Microsoft Dynamics 365 programmes currently underway in Europe, a multi‑year transformation implementing D365 Finance & Operations and Customer Engagement across 60+ entities worldwide.
 



  





 To support this strategic initiative, the organisation is now seeking a Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement Specialist to join its internal digital transformation team based in Stockholm.
 



  





**About the Role** 




  





 The Dynamics 365 CE Specialist will play a key role in the ongoing global rollout of the organisation’s D365 CE solution. With seven entities already live and more than fifty still to follow, this position offers the opportunity to influence the evolution of a worldwide CRM template, collaborate with both internal stakeholders and external consulting partners, and contribute to a long‑term digital roadmap backed by significant investment.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




  




* Support the design, rollout and continuous development of the global D365 CE solution.
* Work closely with business units across multiple regions to gather requirements and improve the global template.
* Collaborate with a major consulting partner and an expanding internal CE/F&O team.
* Contribute to testing, DevOps processes, configuration, integrations and environment management.
* Support Power Platform–based enhancements and solution extensions.
* Help drive adoption and ensure successful implementation across new entities.



  





**What This Opportunity Offers** 




  




* Participation in a high-impact, international transformation spanning 69 global entities.
* Experience within a programme still in full motion, offering long-term progression and learning.
* A chance for a mid-career professional to step into a broader global remit with real influence over direction and future design.
* Exposure to advanced tooling including Power Platform, DevOps, integration frameworks and global template evolution.
* The stability of a long-term digital strategy backed by significant investment.



  





 Interested in applying? Send your application on here or email Eddie on e.leith@tenthrevolution.com for more details

title: Production Engineer - Pharma
description:
At Capgemini Engineering, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world’s most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous cars to life-saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities. Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same.
   

  

**Your Role**
 We are looking for a Production Engineer for our pharmaceutical projects in Stockholm
   

  

*In this role you will play a key role in:*
* Operating independently within your area of expertise and lead others while driving competence development.
* Investigating deviations, support production technical issues, and contribute to improvement projects.
* Manage tasks such as issuing recipes and batch records, coordinating installations, or producing plant layouts and detailed designs.
* Lead or participate in subprojects related to process engineering, automation, or plant design.
* Ensure quality and documentation compliance according to given guidelines.


**Your Profile**
* Master’s or bachelor’s degree in science (or equivalent).
* At least 3 years of experience working in the pharmaceutical industry
* Process development and technology with experience from Process technology, Automation, Facility project planning
* Strong technical proficiency in Process Engineering, Automation, Plant Design, or Project Management.
* Ability to work autonomously and deliver results based on guidelines while assuring quality.
* Experience in leading small projects or subprojects and collaborating across functions such as plant engineers and design engineers.
* Good communication and problem-solving skills to support production and improvement initiatives.


**What We Believe You Will Enjoy About Working Here**
* We recognize the significance of flexible work arrangements to provide support. Be it remote work, or flexible work hours, you will get an environment to maintain healthy work life balance.
* At the heart of our mission is your career growth. Our array of career growth programs and diverse professions are crafted to support you in exploring a world of opportunities.
* We support your personal development and offer an education library with more than 250 000 (!) courses and educations and certifications.


**Application**
 As part of our recruitment process, identity verification or background checks may occasionally be conducted.
   

  

 Valid work permit for Sweden is required.
   

  

 Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.
